import (

// GetNodeSnapshot implements ddxgraphql.StateProvider.
func (s *ServerState) GetNodeSnapshot() ddxgraphql.NodeStateSnapshot {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
	return ddxgraphql.NodeStateSnapshot{
		ID:        s.Node.ID,
		Name:      s.Node.Name,
		StartedAt: s.Node.StartedAt,
		LastSeen:  s.Node.LastSeen,
	}
}

// GetProjectSnapshots implements ddxgraphql.StateProvider.
func (s *ServerState) GetProjectSnapshots(includeUnreachable bool) []*ddxgraphql.Project {
	entries := s.GetProjects(includeUnreachable)
	out := make([]*ddxgraphql.Project, len(entries))
	for i, e := range entries {
		out[i] = projectEntryToGQL(e)
	}
	return out
}

// GetProjectSnapshotByID implements ddxgraphql.StateProvider.
func (s *ServerState) GetProjectSnapshotByID(id string) (*ddxgraphql.Project, bool) {
	entry, ok := s.GetProjectByID(id)
	if !ok {
		return nil, false
	}
	return projectEntryToGQL(entry), true
}

type beadIndexEntry struct {
	ProjectID   string
	ProjectPath string
}

// GetBeadSnapshots implements ddxgraphql.StateProvider.
//
// When projectID is non-empty this delegates to GetBeadSnapshotsForProject so
// the resolver path opens exactly one project's store; a scoped query never
// pays the N-project scan cost (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 1). When projectID is
// empty every registered project is scanned — that is still O(projects) by
// necessity, but each project's bead.Store receives a pushed-down filter so
// non-matching beads are never materialized as BeadSnapshot values.
func (s *ServerState) GetBeadSnapshots(status, label, projectID, search string) []ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot {
	if projectID != "" {
		return s.GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, status, label, search)
	}
	projects := s.GetProjects()
	pred := beadFilterPredicate(status, label, search)
	var result []ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot
	for _, proj := range projects {
		result = append(result, s.beadSnapshotsForProjectEntry(proj, pred)...)
	}
	return result
}

// GetBeadSnapshotsForProject implements ddxgraphql.StateProvider. It opens
// exactly one project's bead store — iteration over other projects is not
// performed for any reason, so the cross-project loop cost is avoided for the
// common per-project bead list.
func (s *ServerState) GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, status, label, search string) []ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot {
	if projectID == "" {
		return nil
	}
	proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)
	if !ok {
		return nil
	}
	pred := beadFilterPredicate(status, label, search)
	return s.beadSnapshotsForProjectEntry(proj, pred)
}

// beadStoreOpenHook is a test-only hook: when non-nil, every call to
// beadSnapshotsForProjectEntry invokes it with the project path it is about
// to open. Tests use this to assert that the scoped path opens exactly one
// project's store (ddx-9ce6842a AC §3).
var beadStoreOpenHook func(projectPath string)

// beadSnapshotsForProjectEntry reads the single project's bead store, applies
// the (possibly nil) predicate at the store layer (filter pushdown), and
// returns GraphQL snapshots for the matching beads. Callers are responsible
// for deciding which projects to pass through — this helper never touches any
// other project's state.
func (s *ServerState) beadSnapshotsForProjectEntry(proj ProjectEntry, pred func(bead.Bead) bool) []ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot {
	if beadStoreOpenHook != nil {
		beadStoreOpenHook(proj.Path)
	}
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(proj.Path, ".ddx"))
	beads, err := store.ReadAllFiltered(pred)
	if err != nil {
		return nil
	}
	out := make([]ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot, 0, len(beads))
	for _, b := range beads {
		s.rememberBeadLocation(b.ID, proj)
		out = append(out, beadSnapshotFromStoreBead(proj.ID, b))
	}
	return out
}

// beadFilterPredicate returns a predicate suitable for bead.Store.ReadAllFiltered
// that applies status/label/search filters. A nil return means "match all".
func beadFilterPredicate(status, label, search string) func(bead.Bead) bool {
	if status == "" && label == "" && search == "" {
		return nil
	}
	q := strings.ToLower(search)
	return func(b bead.Bead) bool {
		if status != "" && b.Status != status {
			return false
		}
		if label != "" && !containsString(b.Labels, label) {
			return false
		}
		if q != "" {
			if !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.ID), q) && !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Title), q) {
				return false
			}
		}
		return true
	}
}

// GetBeadSnapshot implements ddxgraphql.StateProvider.
func (s *ServerState) GetBeadSnapshot(id string) (*ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot, bool) {
	if id == "" {
		return nil, false
	}

	if loc, ok := s.lookupBeadLocation(id); ok {
		proj := ProjectEntry{ID: loc.ProjectID, Path: loc.ProjectPath}
		if snap, ok := readBeadSnapshotFromProject(proj, id); ok {
			return snap, true
		}
		s.forgetBeadLocation(id)
	}

	for _, proj := range s.GetProjects() {
		if snap, ok := readBeadSnapshotFromProject(proj, id); ok {
			s.rememberBeadLocation(id, proj)
			return snap, true
		}
	}
	return nil, false
}

func (s *ServerState) rememberBeadLocation(id string, proj ProjectEntry) {
	if id == "" || proj.ID == "" || proj.Path == "" {
		return
	}
	s.beadIndexMu.Lock()
	defer s.beadIndexMu.Unlock()
	if s.beadIndex == nil {
		s.beadIndex = make(map[string]beadIndexEntry)
	}
	s.beadIndex[id] = beadIndexEntry{ProjectID: proj.ID, ProjectPath: proj.Path}
}

func (s *ServerState) lookupBeadLocation(id string) (beadIndexEntry, bool) {
	s.beadIndexMu.RLock()
	defer s.beadIndexMu.RUnlock()
	if s.beadIndex == nil {
		return beadIndexEntry{}, false
	}
	loc, ok := s.beadIndex[id]
	return loc, ok
}

func (s *ServerState) forgetBeadLocation(id string) {
	s.beadIndexMu.Lock()
	defer s.beadIndexMu.Unlock()
	delete(s.beadIndex, id)
}

func readBeadSnapshotFromProject(proj ProjectEntry, id string) (*ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot, bool) {
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(proj.Path, ".ddx"))
	b, err := store.Get(id)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, false
	}
	snap := beadSnapshotFromStoreBead(proj.ID, *b)
	return &snap, true
}

func beadSnapshotFromStoreBead(projectID string, b bead.Bead) ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot {
	snap := ddxgraphql.BeadSnapshot{
		ProjectID:   projectID,
		ID:          b.ID,
		Title:       b.Title,
		Status:      b.Status,
		Priority:    b.Priority,
		IssueType:   b.IssueType,
		Owner:       b.Owner,
		CreatedAt:   b.CreatedAt,
		CreatedBy:   b.CreatedBy,
		UpdatedAt:   b.UpdatedAt,
		Labels:      b.Labels,
		Parent:      b.Parent,
		Description: b.Description,
		Acceptance:  b.Acceptance,
		Notes:       b.Notes,
	}
	for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
		snap.Dependencies = append(snap.Dependencies, ddxgraphql.BeadDependencySnapshot{
			IssueID:     d.IssueID,
			DependsOnID: d.DependsOnID,
			Type:        d.Type,
			CreatedAt:   d.CreatedAt,
			CreatedBy:   d.CreatedBy,
			Metadata:    d.Metadata,
		})
	}
	return snap
}

func projectEntryToGQL(e ProjectEntry) *ddxgraphql.Project {
	p := &ddxgraphql.Project{
		ID:           e.ID,
		Name:         e.Name,
		Path:         e.Path,
		RegisteredAt: e.RegisteredAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
		LastSeen:     e.LastSeen.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
	}
	if e.GitRemote != "" {
		p.GitRemote = &e.GitRemote
	}
	if e.Unreachable {
		b := true
		p.Unreachable = &b
	}
	if e.TombstonedAt != nil {
		ts := e.TombstonedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
		p.TombstonedAt = &ts
	}
	return p
}

104			if opt != nil {
105				opt(s)
106			}
107		}
108		spec := DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
109		s.File, s.LockDir = spec.PathsUnder(dir)
110	
111		// Set up external backend if configured. bd/br lack per-collection
112		// scoping in their CLI, so non-default collections (e.g. beads-archive)
113		// route through a JSONL fallback the external backend transparently
114		// delegates to. The default collection always goes straight to bd/br
115		// to preserve the existing interchange contract (schema_compat_test.go,
116		// import/export round-trip).
117		switch backendType {
118		case BackendBD, BackendBR:
119			var fallback RawBackend
120			if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
121				fallback = NewJSONLBackend(s.Dir, s.File, s.LockDir, s.LockWait)
122			}
123			if ext, err := NewExternalBackendWithFallback(backendType, s.Collection, fallback); err == nil {
124				s.backend = ext
125			}
126			// Fall through to JSONL if tool not available
127		case BackendAxon:
128			s.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
129		}
130	
131		return s
132	}

134	func storeCallContext(args []any) context.Context {
135		for _, arg := range args {
136			if ctx, ok := arg.(context.Context); ok && ctx != nil {
137				return ctx
138			}
139		}
140		return context.Background()
141	}
142	
143	// NewStoreWithCollection creates a store for a named logical collection.
144	func NewStoreWithCollection(dir, collection string) *Store {
145		return NewStore(dir, WithCollection(collection))
146	}
147	
148	// Init creates the storage directory and file.
149	func (s *Store) Init(args ...any) error {
150		if err := storeCallContext(args).Err(); err != nil {
151			return err
152		}
153		if s.backend != nil {
154			return s.backend.Init()
155		}
156		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
157			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
158		}
159		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
160		if err != nil {
161			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init file: %w", err)
162		}
163		return f.Close()
164	}
165	
166	// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
167	func (s *Store) GenID(args ...any) (string, error) {
168		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
169		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
170			return "", err
171		}
172		prefix := s.Prefix
173		if prefix == "" {
174			prefix = DefaultIDPrefix
175		} else if !strings.HasSuffix(prefix, "-") {
176			prefix += "-"
177		}
178		return RandomHexIDGenerator{Prefix: prefix, Bytes: 4}.GenID(ctx)
179	}

181	// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
182	func (s *Store) ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {
183		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
184		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
185			return nil, err
186		}
187		if s.backend != nil {
188			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
189			if err != nil {
190				return nil, err
191			}
192			return all, nil
193		}
194		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
195		if err != nil {
196			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
197		}
198		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
199		for _, warning := range warnings {
200			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
201		}
202		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
203			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
204			if repairErr != nil {
205				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
206			}
207			if repaired {
208				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
209			}
210		}
211		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
212			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
213		}
214		return beads, nil
215	}

217	// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
218	// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
219	// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
220	// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
221	// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
222	// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
223	// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
224	func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {
225		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
226		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
227			return nil, err
228		}
229		var pred func(Bead) bool
230		for _, arg := range args {
231			if fn, ok := arg.(func(Bead) bool); ok {
232				pred = fn
233				break
234			}
235		}
236		if s.backend != nil {
237			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
238			if err != nil {
239				return nil, err
240			}
241			if pred == nil {
242				return all, nil
243			}
244			out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
245			for _, b := range all {
246				if pred(b) {
247					out = append(out, b)
248				}
249			}"}}

3	import (
4		"context"

7	
8	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
9	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
10	// implement it. New backends should implement Backend directly; RawBackend is
11	// retained for the JSONL/external composition path that Store uses internally.
12	// Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops, events,
13	// archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and are
14	// composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
15	type RawBackend interface {
16		Init() error
17		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
18		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
19		WithLock(fn func() error) error
20	}
21	
22	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
23	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
24	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
25	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
26	// JSONL export/import.
27	//
28	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
29	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
30	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
31	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
32	//
33	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
34	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
35	type Backend interface {
36		// Foundational
37		Init(args ...any) error
38		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
39	
40		// CRUD
41		Create(args ...any) error
42		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
43		Update(args ...any) error
44		Close(args ...any) error
45	
46		// Claim
47		Claim(id, assignee string) error
48		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
49		Unclaim(id string) error
50	
51		// Query
52		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
53		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
54		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
55	
56		// Dep ops
57		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
58		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
59		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
60	
61		// Events
62		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
63		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
64	
65		// Archive split
66		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
67		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
68	
69		// JSONL interchange
70		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
71		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
72	}
73	
74	// TD-027 foundation interfaces. These are additive and intentionally do not
75	// change the legacy Store-backed interface above in this bead slice."}}

1486	// Close sets a bead's status to closed. When the close succeeds and the bead
1487	// carries an inline event history, those events are moved to a sidecar
1488	// attachment under .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl so the active row stays
1489	// small (per ADR-004's attachment model and TD-027 §c).

1505		// Capture last review verdict before closure for reviewer-accuracy tracking.
1506		// If the operator manually closes a BLOCK-reviewed bead, that contradicts
1507		// the reviewer's verdict (potential false-positive).
1508		verdictWas := ""

bead
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"
6	)
7	
8	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
9	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
10	// implement it. New backends should implement Backend directly; RawBackend is
11	// retained for the JSONL/external composition path that Store uses internally.
12	// Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops, events,
13	// archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and are
14	// composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
15	type RawBackend interface {
16		Init() error
17		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
18		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
19		WithLock(fn func() error) error
20	}
21	
22	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
23	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
24	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
25	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
26	// JSONL export/import.
27	//
28	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
29	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
30	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
31	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
32	//
33	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
34	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
35	type Backend interface {
36		// Foundational
37		Init(args ...any) error
38		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
39	
40		// CRUD
41		Create(args ...any) error
42		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
43		Update(args ...any) error
44		Close(args ...any) error
45	
46		// Claim
47		Claim(id, assignee string) error
48		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
49		Unclaim(id string) error
50	
51		// Query
52		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
53		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
54		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
55	
56		// Dep ops
57		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
58		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
59		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
60	
61		// Events
62		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
63		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
64	
65		// Archive split
66		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
67		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
68	
69		// JSONL interchange
70		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
71		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
72	}
73	
74	// TD-027 foundation interfaces. These are additive and intentionally do not
75	// change the legacy Store-backed interface above in this bead slice.
76	type BeadInitializer interface {
77		Init(args ...any) error
78	}
79	
80	type BeadReader interface {
81		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
82		ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
83		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
84	}
85	
86	type BeadLifecycle interface {
87		Create(args ...any) error
88		Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error
89	}
90	
91	type BeadEventReader interface {
92		Events(ctx context.Context, id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
93		EventsByKind(ctx context.Context, id, kind string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
94	}
95	
96	type BeadEventWriter interface {
97		AppendEvent(ctx context.Context, id string, event BeadEvent) error
98	}
99	
100	type BeadQueries interface {
101		List(ctx context.Context, status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
102		Ready(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
103		Blocked(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
104		ReadyExecutionBreakdown(ctx context.Context) (ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
105		ProposedOperatorAttention(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
106		NeedsHuman(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
107		ExternalBlocked(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
108		DependencyWaiting(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
109		BlockedAll(ctx context.Context) ([]BlockedBead, error)
110		Status(ctx context.Context) (*StatusCounts, error)
111	}
112	
113	type BeadDependencyReader interface {
114		DepTree(ctx context.Context, rootID string) (string, error)
115	}
116	
117	type BeadDependencyWriter interface {
118		DepAdd(ctx context.Context, id, depID string) error
119		DepRemove(ctx context.Context, id, depID string) error
120	}
121	
122	type BeadArchive interface {
123		Archive(ctx context.Context, policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
124		Migrate(ctx context.Context) (MigrateStats, error)
125	}
126	
127	type BeadInterchangeReader interface {
128		ExportTo(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer) error
129	}
130	
131	type BeadInterchangeWriter interface {
132		Import(ctx context.Context, source, filePath string) (int, error)
133	}
134	
135	type ReadOnlyBackend interface {
136		BeadInitializer
137		BeadReader
138		BeadEventReader
139		BeadQueries
140		BeadDependencyReader
141		BeadInterchangeReader
142	}
143	
144	// LifecycleSubscriber is the parallel subscription surface from TD-027.
145	type LifecycleSubscriber interface {
146		SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error)
147	}
148	
149	// OperationApplier is an optional fast path for RawBackend implementations
150	// that can preserve specialized mutation behavior.
151	type OperationApplier interface {
152		Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error
153	}
154	
155	// BackendType constants
156	const (
157		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
158		BackendBD    = "bd"
159		BackendBR    = "br"
160	)
161	
162	const DefaultCollection = "beads"
163	"}}

143	// NewStoreWithCollection creates a store for a named logical collection.
144	func NewStoreWithCollection(dir, collection string) *Store {
145		return NewStore(dir, WithCollection(collection))
146	}

148	// Init creates the storage directory and file.
149	func (s *Store) Init(args ...any) error {
150		if err := storeCallContext(args).Err(); err != nil {
151			return err
152		}
153		if s.backend != nil {
154			return s.backend.Init()
155		}
156		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
157			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
158		}
159		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
160		if err != nil {
161			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init file: %w", err)
162		}
163		return f.Close()
164	}

166	// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
167	func (s *Store) GenID(args ...any) (string, error) {
168		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
169		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
170			return "", err
171		}
172		prefix := s.Prefix
173		if prefix == "" {
174			prefix = DefaultIDPrefix
175		} else if !strings.HasSuffix(prefix, "-") {
176			prefix += "-"
177		}
178		return RandomHexIDGenerator{Prefix: prefix, Bytes: 4}.GenID(ctx)
179	}

181	// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
182	func (s *Store) ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {
183		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
184		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
185			return nil, err
186		}
187		if s.backend != nil {
188			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
189			if err != nil {
190				return nil, err
191			}
192			return all, nil
193		}
194		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
195		if err != nil {
196			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
197		}
198		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
199		for _, warning := range warnings {
200			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
201		}
202		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
203			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
204			if repairErr != nil {
205				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
206			}
207			if repaired {
208				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
209			}
210		}
211		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
212			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
213		}
214		return beads, nil
215	}

217	// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
218	// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
219	// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
220	// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
221	// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
222	// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
223	// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
224	func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {

227			return nil, err

233				break

236		if s.backend != nil {

238			if err != nil {
239				return nil, err
240			}
241			if pred == nil {
242				return all, nil
243			}
244			out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
245			for _, b := range all {
246				if pred(b) {
247					out = append(out, b)
248				}
249			}
250			return out, nil
251		}
252		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
253		if err != nil {
254			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
255		}
256		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)

259		}
260		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
261			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
262			if repairErr != nil {
263				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)

265			if repaired {

268		}
269		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
270			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
271		}
272		if pred == nil {
273			return beads, nil
274		}
275		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(beads))
276		for _, b := range beads {
277			if pred(b) {
278				out = append(out, b)
279			}
280		}
281		return out, nil

  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-3fd19af1">
    <title>migrate bead store core APIs to ctx-first signatures</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. Any tests added or updated in `cli/internal/bead/` are named and deterministic, with no caller-package changes in this bead.
    </description>

.ddx/executions/20260602T004728-1dfb822a/manifest.json:11:    "title": "migrate bead store core APIs to ctx-first signatures",
.ddx/executions/20260602T004728-1dfb822a/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. Any tests added or updated in `cli/internal/bead/` are named and deterministic, with no caller-package changes in this bead.",

reason=bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885): 1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead"; "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.") and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead." 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.

Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885):

1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go):
     Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149
     GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167
     ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182
     ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224
     Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518
     Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598
     Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627
     Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426
   And in archive.go:
     GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172
   Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts
   either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today.

2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first
   parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in
   cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying
   AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`,
   etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in
   external packages.

3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external
   callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead";
   "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent,
   cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.")
   and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead."

4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs
   `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129).
   That build covers every package in the module, including all the
   external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching.

5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today
   (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`):
     - 174 Go files reference these Store methods.
     - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg
       `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37,
       cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/...,
       cli/internal/server/...).
     - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too:
         cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0])
         cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...)
   `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one
   of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/
   GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is
   no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current
   call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a
   single receiver type).

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that
   preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170
   files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server,
   internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the
   "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That
   is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split
   (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason.

Operator action requested:

  Re-scope this bead to one of:

  (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE
      block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the
      original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if
      pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd,
      cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server,
      cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics.

  (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test
      ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and
      explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit
      will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The
      follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is
      complete.

  (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or
      retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first
      Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the
      legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration",
      then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the
      caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027
      (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package
      first) without breaking the build outside the package.

.ddx/executions/20260602T004728-1dfb822a/prompt.md:6:    <title>migrate bead store core APIs to ctx-first signatures</title>
.ddx/executions/20260602T004728-1dfb822a/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. Any tests added or updated in `cli/internal/bead/` are named and deterministic, with no caller-package changes in this bead.

10	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadMigrateCommand() *cobra.Command {
11		cmd := &cobra.Command{
12			Use:   "migrate",
13			Short: "Split active beads.jsonl into beads + beads-archive + attachments",
14			Long: `Split the existing .ddx/beads.jsonl into the modern layout:
15	
16	  * Closed beads' inline events are moved to per-bead sidecars under
17	    .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl (ADR-004).
18	  * Eligible closed beads are moved to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl using a
19	    permissive archival policy (TD-027).
20	
21	The command is idempotent — a second run with no further changes is a
22	no-op. All bead IDs remain addressable: 'ddx bead show', 'ddx bead list',
23	and 'ddx bead status' transparently consult the archive partner.`,
24			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
25			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
26				s := f.beadStore()
27				dryRun, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
28				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
29	
30				var (
31					ext  int
32					arch int
33				)
34				if dryRun {
35					st, err := s.MigrateDryRun()
36					if err != nil {
37						return err
38					}
39					ext, arch = st.EventsExternalized, st.Archived
40				} else {
41					st, err := s.Migrate()
42					if err != nil {
43						return err
44					}
45					ext, arch = st.EventsExternalized, st.Archived
46					if st.Changed() {
47						f.beadAutoCommit(fmt.Sprintf("migrate: externalize=%d archive=%d", ext, arch))
48					}
49				}
50				stats := migrateStatsView{EventsExternalized: ext, Archived: arch, DryRun: dryRun}
51	
52				if asJSON {
53					enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
54					enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
55					return enc.Encode(stats)
56				}
57				out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
58				if dryRun {
59					fmt.Fprintln(out, "Dry run — no files were modified.")
60				}
61				fmt.Fprintf(out, "Externalized events: %d\n", ext)
62				fmt.Fprintf(out, "Archived beads:      %d\n", arch)
63				if ext == 0 && arch == 0 {
64					fmt.Fprintln(out, "No changes — already migrated.")
65				}
66				return nil
67			},

2	ddx:
3	  id: TD-030
4	  depends_on:
5	    - ADR-004
6	    - SD-004
7	    - FEAT-004
8	  status: draft
9	---
10	# Technical Design: Axon Bead-Tracker Backend
11	
12	## Status
13	
14	Draft. Specifies the integration between the DDx bead tracker
15	(FEAT-004 / SD-004 / ADR-004) and an external Axon entity-graph-relational
16	store as a pluggable storage backend behind the
17	`bead_tracker.backend` switch introduced in TD-027.
18	
19	## Background
20	
21	Source plan: `/tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md`. That document captured the
22	field-by-field schema mapping, operation mapping, migration plan, and
23	open questions that fed the operator review. The locked decisions from
24	that review are baked into this TD; the deferred-default decisions are
25	adopted as v1 policy below. This TD does not relitigate those decisions
26	— it polishes them into an implementable design.
27	
28	Axon is a separate Rust project (`/home/erik/Projects/axon`) exposing an
29	entity-graph-relational store over gRPC, REST, GraphQL, and MCP.
30	Backing stores are pluggable per deployment (PostgreSQL, SQLite,
31	FoundationDB, fjall) per Axon SPIKE-001. Axon ships an opinionated
32	`bead.rs` module with its own `BeadStatus` state machine; **DDx does
33	not consume that module** — DDx beads have a different status set
34	(`open`/`in_progress`/`closed`/`blocked` plus claim semantics), an
35	append-only events array, and bd/br JSONL interchange requirements that
36	do not match Axon's built-in shape. DDx instead defines its own
37	collection (`ddx_beads`) and JSON Schema and uses Axon as a generic
38	typed-entity store.
39	
40	## Locked decisions
41	
42	These are operator-approved and not negotiable inside this TD:
43	
44	| # | Decision | Implication for design |
45	|---|---|---|
46	| D1 | **Deployment**: separate axon-server, operator-managed. | DDx does **not** spawn or supervise Axon. `ddx-server` has no responsibility for Axon's lifecycle. The operator runs Axon via systemd / docker-compose / etc. and configures the connection string in `.ddx/config.yaml`. `ddx doctor` gains an Axon-reachability diagnostic. |
47	| D2 | **Auth**: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. | Reuses ADR-006 (tsnet authentication). No new auth machinery in DDx. For remote Axon endpoints, DDx dials through a tsnet listener; for local Axon, plaintext gRPC on `127.0.0.1` is acceptable. |
48	| D3 | **Events storage**: separate `ddx_bead_events` collection with `event_of` links (Option B). | Two collections: `ddx_beads` (the bead row) and `ddx_bead_events` (one entity per event). Bead row stays light; events scale per-bead independently. |
49	| D4 | **Offline posture**: refuse all bead ops with a clear error when Axon is unreachable. | Axon is the source of truth. No local read-through cache, no WAL, no degraded-mode reads. `ddx bead *` and `ddx work` fail loudly with `axon unreachable at <addr>: <error>; check connectivity / config`. |
50	
51	## v1 policy defaults (deferred decisions)
52	
53	These can be revisited if implementation surfaces a problem; they are
54	not load-bearing for the architecture:
55	
56	| Question | v1 policy | Rationale |
57	|---|---|---|
58	| Schema versioning | **Lazy migrate on read.** When DDx reads an entity at an older `schema_version`, it transforms in-memory, returns to the caller, and writes back at the new version on the next mutation. A future `ddx bead schema upgrade` command exists for breaking changes. | Avoids large eager-migrate batches; standard pattern. |
59	| `ddx bead ready` query | **Two-phase**: `QueryEntities(status=open)`, then per-candidate `Traverse(depends_on, outgoing)` to check whether each dep is closed. Benchmark on the 1100-bead archive scale (see Test plan §); if too slow, escalate to a server-side computed view (see Open Questions for Axon §1) or a derived `is_ready` index field maintained at write time. | Simplest correct implementation with a clear performance escalation path. |
60	| Backup / DR | **Operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.** `ddx bead export` is the documented logical backup format. | Avoids DDx duplicating Axon's DR concerns. Aligns with operator-managed deployment (D1). |
61	
62	## Architectural decision
63	
64	DDx defines a `ddx_beads` collection (and a companion `ddx_bead_events`
65	collection per D3) in Axon, each with a DDx-controlled JSON Schema
66	registered via Axon `PutSchema`. Axon serves as a generic
67	entity-graph-relational store; bead semantics (state machine, claim
68	flow, ready-queue computation) live in `cli/internal/bead`. Axon's
69	opinionated `bead.rs` is informational only.
70	
71	Rationale (from source plan, recorded for the record):
72	
73	- Preserves the DDx schema (FEAT-004 / SD-004) without forcing Axon's
74	  status model onto DDx.
75	- Maintains bd/br interchange compatibility — `ddx bead export | bd
76	  import` round-trip is codified in
77	  `cli/internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go`.
78	- Lets HELIX and other plugins extend the bead `Extra` map without
79	  coordinating with Axon's `bead.rs` schema.
80	- DDx ships per-collection schemas as artifacts under
81	  `cli/internal/bead/schema/` and pushes them via `PutSchema` on first
82	  use.
83	
84	## Wire transport
85	
86	Use **gRPC**. DDx generates a Go client from `axon.proto` via
87	`protoc-gen-go` + `protoc-gen-go-grpc`. The generated client lives at
88	`cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/` with a `make gen-axon-client` target;
89	`axon.proto` is checked in alongside its sha256 sum so consumer/producer
90	drift surfaces in CI. REST and MCP are not used; they remain available
91	fallbacks if gRPC adoption proves operationally heavy.
92	
93	Axon version pin: the build pins a specific Axon release tag (e.g.
94	`axon@v0.x.y`) and tracks Axon's CHANGELOG. Axon is pre-release; wire
95	protocol and schema model are not yet frozen, so the pin is mandatory.
96	
97	## Schema mapping
98	
99	DDx bead record (per `cli/internal/bead/marshal.go` and
100	`cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json`) → Axon entity in
101	collection `ddx_beads`:
102	
103	| DDx bead field | Axon mapping |
104	|---|---|
105	| `id` | Axon `EntityId` (string; preserves the existing `ddx-<8hex>` form) |
106	| `title` | `data_json.title` |
107	| `description` | `data_json.description` |
108	| `acceptance` | `data_json.acceptance` |
109	| `issue_type` (task/bug/epic/chore) | `data_json.issue_type` (string enum) |
110	| `status` (open/in_progress/closed/blocked) | `data_json.status` (string enum) — validated by the registered JSON Schema; **not** routed through Axon's `TransitionLifecycle` (DDx owns the state machine) |
111	| `priority`, `labels`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `assignee`, `claimed_at`, `claimed_pid` | `data_json.*` (verbatim field-for-field) |
112	| `parent` | Axon **link** of type `parent`, child → parent entity |
113	| `dependencies` | Axon **links** of type `depends_on`, this → each dependency target |
114	| `events` | per D3, **Option B**: each event is its own entity in `ddx_bead_events`, linked back to the bead via an `event_of` link. The bead row's `data_json` does **not** carry the events array. Export materialises the inline events array for bd/br interchange (see Operation mapping). |
115	| `Extra` (HELIX/plugin custom fields) | `data_json.extra` with `additionalProperties: true` at that path; preserves round-trip |
116	| `version` (optimistic concurrency) | `EntityProto.version` from Axon (returned on every Get; sent as `expected_version` on Update) |
117	
118	### Schema artifacts
119	
120	Two new schema files, both under `cli/internal/bead/schema/`:
121	
122	- `bead-record-axon.schema.json` — extends `bead-record.schema.json`,
123	  registered as the schema for collection `ddx_beads`.
124	- `bead-event-axon.schema.json` — schema for collection
125	  `ddx_bead_events`. Fields: `event_id`, `at`, `kind`, `payload`, plus
126	  `event_of` link target.
127	
128	Both schemas carry an explicit `schema_version` (starts at `1`).
129	Schema upgrades push a new version via `PutSchema`; live entities
130	migrate lazily on read per the v1 policy.
131	
132	## Operation mapping
133	
134	| DDx operation | Axon RPC(s) |
135	|---|---|
136	| `ddx bead create` | `CreateEntity(collection=ddx_beads)` — wrapped in `CommitTransaction` if the same call also creates dep links |
137	| `ddx bead show <id>` | `GetEntity` for the bead row + `Traverse(depends_on, outgoing)` + `Traverse(parent, outgoing)` + `Traverse(event_of, incoming)` and `GetEntity` per event entity. May escalate to a single server-side aggregation if Axon adds one — see Open Questions for Axon §1. |
138	| `ddx bead update` | `UpdateEntity` with `expected_version` (OCC) |
139	| `ddx bead close` | `UpdateEntity` (status → closed) — may include the closing event-write in the same `CommitTransaction` |
140	| `ddx bead claim` | `UpdateEntity` (status → in_progress + `claimed_at` + `claimed_pid`) — atomic via OCC: on version mismatch, the claim lost the race and the caller retries (or surfaces "claimed by another worker") |
141	| `ddx bead ready` | per v1 policy: `QueryEntities(status=open)` then per-candidate `Traverse(depends_on, outgoing)` checking dependency status; escalation paths documented above |
142	| `ddx bead blocked` | symmetric to ready: query `status=open` then filter to those with at least one non-closed dependency |
143	| `ddx bead dep add/remove` | `CreateLink` / `DeleteLink` (link type `depends_on`) |
144	| `ddx bead dep tree` | `Traverse(depends_on, outgoing, transitive)` |
145	| `ddx bead evidence add` | `CreateEntity(collection=ddx_bead_events)` + `CreateLink(event_of)` in one `CommitTransaction` |
146	| `ddx bead export` | iterate `QueryEntities(collection=ddx_beads)`, fan-out to events via `Traverse(event_of, incoming)`, materialise the inline events array, marshal to bd/br JSONL — must round-trip per FEAT-004 + `schema_compat_test.go` |
147	| `ddx bead import` | reverse: parse JSONL, split into bead row + event entities, write each bead inside a transaction |
148	
149	### Concurrency model
150	
151	Axon's `expected_version` (OCC) plus `CommitTransaction` (ACID over
152	batched ops) replace **both** of the current JSONL backend's
153	serialization mechanisms:
154	
155	- the `flock` on `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (acquired via
156	  `JSONLBackend.WithLock` per `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:74`)
157	- the git tracker-commit lock that motivated bead `ddx-da11a34a` (the
158	  v1 patch in `cli/internal/bead/lock.go` serializing
159	  `git add .ddx/beads.jsonl && git commit`)
160	
161	Multi-worker concurrency falls out for free — each DDx worker is just
162	another gRPC client. The `git add .ddx/beads.jsonl && git commit` step
163	retires entirely under the Axon backend: bead state lives in Axon, not
164	on disk under git.
165	
166	### Multi-machine story (Story 14 prerequisite)
167	
168	FEAT-026 / Story 14 (per ADR-007 federation topology) needs a single
169	authoritative bead store that multiple machines can read and mutate
170	without git as the synchronisation channel. The JSONL backend cannot
171	satisfy that contract — it relies on a shared filesystem and a single
172	git remote as the merge point, and ADR-021 already calls out that the
173	Axon path "gets the right answer for free" for the multi-node story.
174	This TD is the prerequisite work: once `bead_tracker.backend: axon` is
175	the configured backend, every DDx worker on every machine speaks gRPC
176	to the same Axon endpoint and OCC handles cross-machine races
177	identically to single-machine cross-process races. No new federation
178	machinery is needed inside DDx; FEAT-026 reduces to "operators run one
179	Axon and point all `.ddx/config.yaml` files at it" plus the auth story
180	in D2 (localhost / tsnet via ADR-006).
181	
182	## Deployment and configuration
183	
184	`.ddx/config.yaml` schema additions:
185	
186	```yaml
187	bead_tracker:
188	  backend: axon            # one of: jsonl (current default), bd, axon
189	  axon:
190	    address: localhost:50051     # gRPC endpoint (host:port)
191	    database: ddx-default        # Axon database name
192	    namespace: project-<name>    # one Axon namespace per DDx project
193	    collection: ddx_beads        # default; configurable
194	    events_collection: ddx_bead_events
195	    schema_version: 1
196	    auth:
197	      mode: localhost            # 'localhost' | 'tsnet'
198	      tsnet:                     # only when mode=tsnet, per ADR-006
199	        hostname: ddx-<project>
200	        # remaining tsnet fields per ADR-006
201	```
202	
203	`auth.mode: localhost` requires `address` to resolve to a loopback
204	host; DDx refuses to dial non-loopback addresses without `mode: tsnet`
205	(matches the security stance in `concerns.md`).
206	
207	### `ddx doctor` diagnostic
208	
209	`ddx doctor` adds a `bead-tracker` section. When `backend: axon` is
210	configured, the diagnostic:
211	
212	1. Resolves `address` and dials the gRPC endpoint with a 2s timeout.
213	2. Calls `DescribeCollection(ddx_beads)` and `DescribeCollection(ddx_bead_events)`.
214	3. Verifies that the registered schema version matches the configured
215	   `schema_version`.
216	4. Surfaces actionable errors: unreachable, collection missing, schema
217	   missing, schema version mismatch.
218	
219	Failure of the doctor check does **not** automatically initialize Axon
220	state — operators run `ddx bead migrate --to=axon` (see Migration plan)
221	which is responsible for collection/schema bring-up.
222	
223	## Migration plan
224	
225	Implements bead `ddx-155204fd` (B_MIGRATE). Command:
226	`ddx bead migrate --to=axon`.
227	
228	1. **Pre-flight**: dial Axon; verify or `PutSchema` for both
229	   collections; `CreateCollection` if absent.
230	2. **Read source**: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`,
231	   and `.ddx/attachments/*` (externalised events from
232	   `ddx bead migrate` under the JSONL backend, per ADR-004).
233	3. **Per bead**, in one `CommitTransaction`:
234	   - `CreateEntity(collection=ddx_beads)` with the mapped data
235	   - `CreateLink(depends_on)` for each dependency
236	   - `CreateLink(parent)` if applicable
237	   - For each event: `CreateEntity(collection=ddx_bead_events)` +
238	     `CreateLink(event_of)`
239	4. **Verify**: pre-migration bead count = post-migration count; sample
240	   N=20 random beads and hash-compare `data_json` plus traversed link
241	   sets.
242	5. **On success**: rename `.ddx/beads.jsonl` →
243	   `.ddx/beads.jsonl.pre-axon-migration.bak` (retained for the rollback
244	   window; not deleted).
245	6. **Switch** `bead_tracker.backend: axon` in `.ddx/config.yaml`.
246	
247	The migration is idempotent: re-running on a partially or fully
248	migrated store is a no-op via OCC version checks plus presence checks
249	on the target entity ids.
250	
251	## Test plan
252	
253	1. **Backend conformance**: parameterise the existing
254	   `cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go` suite (10+ chaos tests) by backend
255	   selector (`jsonl`, `axon`); the Axon-backed runs must pass identically.
256	2. **Unit/integration** under `cli/internal/bead/axon/`:
257	   - `integration_test.go`: round-trip create → get → update → claim →
258	     close → re-read against a real Axon test instance
259	   - concurrent-claim race: two goroutines race a claim on the same
260	     bead; exactly one wins; the loser observes a version mismatch
261	   - dependency add/remove with `Traverse` verification
262	   - export → bd-shape JSONL → import round-trip; assert byte-equal
263	     after a re-export (per `schema_compat_test.go`)
264	3. **End-to-end**: spawn 2 DDx workers against a shared Axon, queue 50
265	   trivial beads, both drain to closure with no contention errors —
266	   replaces the workaround in `ddx-da11a34a`.
267	4. **Performance benchmark**: queue ops over the existing 1100-bead
268	   archive-scale fixture; assert parity-or-better against the JSONL
269	   backend on `bead ready`, `bead show`, and `bead create`. Failure
270	   here triggers the v1-policy escalation path for `bead ready`
271	   (server-side view or derived index).
272	5. **Doctor diagnostic**: `ddx doctor` surfaces each of the four
273	   failure modes (unreachable, missing collection, missing schema,
274	   schema version mismatch) with the expected message.
275	6. **Offline posture (D4)**: with Axon stopped, every `ddx bead *`
276	   subcommand and `ddx work` exit non-zero with the
277	   `axon unreachable at <addr>` message; no local writes occur.
278	
279	## Backend interface (Go)
280	
281	The current `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` interface is too coarse for
282	Axon — `WriteAll(beads []Bead)` cannot reasonably be implemented
283	against a remote entity store, and `WithLock` assumes a process-local
284	mutex around an in-memory rewrite. This TD replaces the existing
285	interface with a per-entity surface that both backends can satisfy.
286	Existing JSONL semantics are preserved by a thin adapter that wraps
287	the existing `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`/`flock` pair.
288	
289	```go
290	// cli/internal/bead/backend.go
291	package bead
292	
293	import "context"
294	
295	// Backend is the storage contract for the bead tracker. Both the
296	// JSONL backend (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go) and the axon
297	// backend (cli/internal/bead/axon/) implement this surface. The
298	// chaos tests under cli/internal/bead/ run against any registered
299	// Backend via newTestStore(t, BackendName).
300	type Backend interface {
301	    // Init prepares storage (create JSONL file + lock dir; or for
302	    // axon: ensure collections exist and registered schema is current).
303	    Init(ctx context.Context) error
304	
305	    // Get returns one bead with its OCC version. ErrNotFound for missing.
306	    Get(ctx context.Context, id string) (Bead, Version, error)
307	
308	    // List returns all beads in the tracker collection (open + in_progress
309	    // + closed not yet archived). Order is unspecified.
310	    List(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
311	
312	    // Create persists a new bead. Bead.ID may be empty; backend assigns
313	    // a ddx-<8hex> id and returns the assigned id. Fails if id is set
314	    // and already exists.
315	    Create(ctx context.Context, b Bead) (string, Version, error)
316	
317	    // Update applies mutate to the bead identified by id under
318	    // optimistic concurrency control: read at version V, apply mutate,
319	    // write iff stored version == V. ErrVersionMismatch on conflict;
320	    // caller retries. Used by claim, status transitions, and any
321	    // field edit. Backend MUST serialize concurrent Update calls on
322	    // the same id (jsonl: via flock; axon: via expected_version).
323	    Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead) error) error
324	
325	    // AppendEvent records an immutable audit event linked to a bead.
326	    // For jsonl: appends to the inline events array under the same
327	    // lock as the bead row. For axon: CreateEntity in
328	    // ddx_bead_events + CreateLink(event_of) in one CommitTransaction.
329	    AppendEvent(ctx context.Context, beadID string, ev Event) error
330	
331	    // AddDep / RemoveDep maintain the depends_on graph. For jsonl:
332	    // mutate Dependencies on the bead row. For axon: CreateLink /
333	    // DeleteLink with type=depends_on.
334	    AddDep(ctx context.Context, fromID, toID string) error
335	    RemoveDep(ctx context.Context, fromID, toID string) error
336	
337	    // Ready returns beads with Status==open whose every dependency
338	    // is Status==closed. Implementations are free to compute this
339	    // server-side; the JSONL implementation does it client-side.
340	    Ready(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
341	
342	    // Close is sugar for Update(id, b -> b.Status = closed) plus
343	    // AppendEvent(closed). Provided so the backend can fuse both
344	    // writes into a single transaction (axon) or single locked
345	    // rewrite (jsonl).
346	    Close(ctx context.Context, id string) error
347	
348	    // Export streams beads in bd/br JSONL interchange shape (events
349	    // materialised inline). Round-trip with Import is verified by
350	    // schema_compat_test.go.
351	    Export(ctx context.Context, w BeadWriter) error
352	    Import(ctx context.Context, r BeadReader) error
353	}
354	
355	// Version is an opaque OCC token (jsonl: monotonic int from the
356	// in-memory snapshot; axon: EntityProto.version).
357	type Version uint64
358	
359	var (
360	    ErrNotFound        = errors.New("bead: not found")
361	    ErrVersionMismatch = errors.New("bead: version mismatch (concurrent update)")
362	    ErrBackendOffline  = errors.New("bead: backend unreachable")
363	)
364	```
365	
366	The `BackendType` constants in `backend.go` extend with
367	`BackendAxon = "axon"`. The factory in `cli/internal/bead/registry.go`
368	selects the implementation from `bead_tracker.backend` in
369	`.ddx/config.yaml`. The existing `JSONLBackend` becomes an internal
370	adapter behind this interface; call sites in `cli/internal/bead/` and
371	`cli/cmd/bead_*.go` migrate to the new methods (mechanical change —
372	the operations already exist, just on `Store` rather than `Backend`).
373	
374	## Chaos contract the new backend must satisfy
375	
376	The contract under `cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go` is the
377	parameterised conformance suite for any `Backend` implementation. The
378	tests already operate on a `Store` value via `newTestStore(t)`; this TD
379	parameterises that helper by backend selector
380	(`newTestStore(t, BackendJSONL)` / `newTestStore(t, BackendAxon)`) and
381	both runs MUST pass identically. The following invariants are the
382	contract surface:
383	
384	| Test | Invariant the backend must guarantee |
385	|---|---|
386	| `TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety` | 100 concurrent `Create` calls from 10 goroutines all succeed and all 100 beads are present afterwards with unique ids. No duplicate ids, no lost writes, no torn rows. |
387	| `TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions` | 5 goroutines × 20 cycles of `Update(status)` on the same bead leave the bead with a valid status (`open` / `in_progress` / `closed`) and the store fully readable. Transient `ErrVersionMismatch` is acceptable (caller's retry concern); corruption is not. |
388	| `TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend` | A `Close(idA)` racing a `Create(B)` never loses either op: B exists with the right title; A's row remains parseable. Reproduces the original JSONL bug; required of every backend. |
389	| `TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost` | After a `Close(idA)` racing a `Create(B)` completes, `Get(idA).Status == closed` deterministically. The backend MUST serialize the read-modify-write of A (jsonl: flock; axon: OCC + retry on mismatch). The "close is never lost" invariant is the strict regression test for the JSONL bug and the acceptance criterion for axon. |
390	| `TestChaos_JSONLRoundTripIntegrity` | Renamed to `TestChaos_RoundTripIntegrity` once parameterised. Beads with unicode, emoji, embedded JSON-injection bytes, tabs, multi-line descriptions, and 200/1000/500-char field maxima survive `Create → ReadAll` field-for-field (Title, Description, Notes, Priority, Status, Labels). Validates that the wire format and schema preserve all `Bead` fields without truncation or escape-sequence loss. |
391	| `TestChaos_*` (all of the above) | No partial writes are visible to a concurrent reader. For jsonl this is the `writeAtomicFile` + `flock` guarantee; for axon this is the `CommitTransaction` ACID guarantee. |
392	
393	Companion suites that the axon backend must also pass once
394	parameterised: `chaos2_test.go`, `property_test.go`, `fuzz_test.go`,
395	`merge_test.go`, and `schema_compat_test.go` (the bd/br JSONL
396	round-trip). `schema_compat_test.go` is the strictest
397	interoperability check — `Export` then `Import` then `Export` must
398	produce byte-equal output regardless of backend.
399	
400	## Archive policy under axon
401	
402	ADR-004 specifies that closed-bead events are externalised to
403	`.ddx/attachments/` and that eligible closed beads move to
404	`.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` to keep the active tracker file small. Under
405	the axon backend the externalisation problem disappears: events are
406	already separate entities in `ddx_bead_events` (per D3), so the active
407	`ddx_beads` row never carries the events array on the wire. The
408	"archive" distinction collapses to a soft index: `ddx bead migrate`
409	under the axon backend is a no-op for archive-split purposes; closed
410	beads remain in the same `ddx_beads` collection, distinguished only by
411	`status=closed`. Queries that historically scanned only the active
412	file (`bead list`, `bead ready`) become `QueryEntities` calls with a
413	`status` filter and pay nothing for closed-bead volume. The
414	attachments mechanism remains the canonical location for non-event
415	artifacts (large logs, screenshots) referenced from event payloads;
416	nothing in this TD changes the attachments contract.
417	
418	| Current pain (JSONL backend) | Axon backend fix |
419	|---|---|
420	| Multi-worker `.git/index.lock` contention (bead `ddx-da11a34a`) | No JSONL writes, no git tracker commits — gone. |
421	| `beads.jsonl > 5 MB` lefthook complications | No JSONL on disk; growth is an Axon-side concern. |
422	| ADR-004 archive split + attachment store complexity | Subsumed by the two-collection model + per-event entities. |
423	| Multi-machine sync (today is git-based per FEAT-023) | Axon server is the source of truth; workers are clients. Federation falls out. |
424	| Implicit schema enforcement | Axon validates per-entity against the registered JSON Schema. |
425	| Audit trail is event-array-on-bead | Events are first-class entities; Axon's per-entity immutable audit (`QueryAuditByEntity`) augments. |
426	| OCC is flock-only | OCC via `expected_version` end-to-end. |
427	
428	## Out of scope
429	
430	- The git tracker-commit lock issue (`ddx-da11a34a`) becomes moot under
431	  the Axon backend, but the lock-fix bead remains relevant for the
432	  JSONL backend's lifetime as the fallback path.
433	- Axon's pre-release status: wire protocol, data model, and SDK surface
434	  are not frozen. DDx pins a specific Axon version and tracks the
435	  CHANGELOG.
436	- Axon's Go SDK does not exist; DDx generates its own from `axon.proto`.
437	
438	## Open questions for the Axon team
439	
440	These are filed as cross-repo coordination items; each can land as an
441	issue against the Axon repo. They do not block this TD from shipping —
442	the v1 policy defaults are sufficient until benchmarks or operator
443	reports surface a concrete need.
444	
445	1. **Server-side aggregation for the ready-queue traversal pattern.**
446	   Is there (or could there be) an Axon RPC or computed-view that
447	   answers "all entities in collection C with status=S whose outgoing
448	   links of type T all point to entities with status ∈ {S'}" in a
449	   single round-trip? The v1 policy uses two-phase client-side
450	   traversal; a server-side answer would unlock larger archives.
451	2. **ts-net alignment with ADR-006.** Does Axon's auth/transport story
452	   support a Tailscale tsnet listener directly, or does DDx need to
453	   wrap the gRPC dial through its own tsnet socket? Either is workable;
454	   the answer determines whether ADR-006's tsnet integration extends
455	   transparently or requires adapter code on the DDx side.

Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
//
// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
//
// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
// reference in the event body.
//
// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
// matches the scanner in the agent package.
package bead

import (
	"bufio"
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
