// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
type Store struct {
	Collection string
	Dir        string
	File       string
	Prefix     string
	LockDir    string
	LockWait   time.Duration
	backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
}

// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
// cli/cmd callers program against.
var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)

type StoreOption func(*Store)

// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
	return func(s *Store) {
		if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
			s.Collection = cleaned
		}
	}
}

// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
	if dir == "" {
		dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	}
	workingDir := dir
	if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
		workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
	}
	prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
	var configBackend string
	if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
		if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
			prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
		}
		configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
	}
	if prefix == "" {
		prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
	}
	// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
	backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
	if backendType == "" {
		backendType = configBackend
	}
	if backendType == "" {
		backendType = BackendJSONL
	}

	s := &Store{
		Collection: DefaultCollection,
		Dir:        dir,
		Prefix:     prefix,
		LockWait:   parseDurationOr("DDX_BEAD_LOCK_TIMEOUT", 10*time.Second),
	}
	for _, opt := range opts {
		if opt != nil {
			opt(s)
		}
	}
	spec := DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
	s.File, s.LockDir = spec.PathsUnder(dir)

	// Set up external backend if configured. bd/br lack per-collection
	// scoping in their CLI, so non-default collections (e.g. beads-archive)
	// route through a JSONL fallback the external backend transparently
	// delegates to. The default collection always goes straight to bd/br
	// to preserve the existing interchange contract (schema_compat_test.go,
	// import/export round-trip).
	switch backendType {
	case BackendBD, BackendBR:
		var fallback RawBackend
		if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
			fallback = NewJSONLBackend(s.Dir, s.File, s.LockDir, s.LockWait)
		}
		if ext, err := NewExternalBackendWithFallback(backendType, s.Collection, fallback); err == nil {
			s.backend = ext
		}
		// Fall through to JSONL if tool not available
	case BackendAxon:
		s.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
	}

	return s
}

func storeCallContext(args []any) context.Context {
	for _, arg := range args {
		if ctx, ok := arg.(context.Context); ok && ctx != nil {
			return ctx
		}
	}
	return context.Background()
}

// NewStoreWithCollection creates a store for a named logical collection.
func NewStoreWithCollection(dir, collection string) *Store {
	return NewStore(dir, WithCollection(collection))
}

// Init creates the storage directory and file.
func (s *Store) Init(ctx context.Context) error {
	if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if s.backend != nil {
		return s.backend.Init()
	}
	if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
	}
	f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("bead: init file: %w", err)
	}
	return f.Close()
}

// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
func (s *Store) GenID(ctx context.Context) (string, error) {
	if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	prefix := s.Prefix
	if prefix == "" {
		prefix = DefaultIDPrefix
	} else if !strings.HasSuffix(prefix, "-") {
		prefix += "-"
	}
	return RandomHexIDGenerator{Prefix: prefix, Bytes: 4}.GenID(ctx)
}

// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
func (s *Store) ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {
	ctx := storeCallContext(args)
	if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if s.backend != nil {
		all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		return all, nil
	}
	beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
	}
	beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
	for _, warning := range warnings {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
	}
	if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
		repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
		if repairErr != nil {
			return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
		}
		if repaired {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
		}
	}
	if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
	}
	return beads, nil
}

// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {
	ctx := storeCallContext(args)
	if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	var pred func(Bead) bool
	for _, arg := range args {
		if fn, ok := arg.(func(Bead) bool); ok {
			pred = fn
			break
		}
	}
	if s.backend != nil {
		all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		if pred == nil {
			return all, nil
		}
		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
		for _, b := range all {
			if pred(b) {
				out = append(out, b)
			}
		}
		return out, nil
	}
	beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
	}
	beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
	for _, warning := range warnings {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
	}
	if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
		repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()

// TestMain scrubs all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
// test subprocesses don't inherit lefthook's GIT_DIR/GIT_WORK_TREE and leak
// into the parent repo's config.
func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
	for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
		if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
			if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
				_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
			}
		}
	}
	os.Exit(m.Run())
}

func newTestStore(t *testing.T) *Store {
	t.Helper()
	dir := t.TempDir()
	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, s.Init(context.Background()))
	return s
}

func newConfiguredStore(t *testing.T, backend string) *Store {
	t.Helper()
	dir := t.TempDir()
	if backend != "" {
		writeStoreConfig(t, dir, backend)
	}
	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, s.Init(context.Background()))
	return s
}

func newJSONLStore(t *testing.T) *Store {
	t.Helper()
	return newConfiguredStore(t, BackendJSONL)
}

func writeStoreConfig(t *testing.T, dir string, backend string) {
	t.Helper()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	content := fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
bead:
  backend: %s
`, backend)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(content), 0o644))
}

func TestInit(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, s.Init(context.Background()))

	_, err := os.Stat(s.File)
	assert.NoError(t, err, "beads.jsonl should exist after init")
}

func TestInitUsesCollectionFile(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	s := NewStoreWithCollection(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName), "exec-runs")
	require.Equal(t, "exec-runs", s.Collection)
	require.Equal(t, filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName, "exec-runs.jsonl"), s.File)
	require.Equal(t, filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName, "exec-runs.lock"), s.LockDir)
	require.NoError(t, s.Init(context.Background()))

	_, err := os.Stat(s.File)
	assert.NoError(t, err, "collection file should exist after init")
}

func TestStoreInit_RejectsCanceledContext(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	cancel()
	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ddxroot.DirName))
	err := s.Init(ctx)
	require.Error(t, err, "Init must return an error when context is already canceled")
}

func TestStoreGenID_RejectsCanceledContext(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	cancel()
	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ddxroot.DirName))
	_, err := s.GenID(ctx)
	require.Error(t, err, "GenID must return an error when context is already canceled")
}

func TestStoreCreate_RejectsCanceledContext(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	cancel()
	s := newTestStore(t)
	err := s.Create(ctx, &Bead{Title: "canceled create"})
	require.Error(t, err, "Create must return an error when context is already canceled")
}

func TestStoreUpdate_RejectsCanceledContext(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	cancel()
	s := newTestStore(t)
	err := s.Update(ctx, "missing", func(*Bead) {})
	require.Error(t, err, "Update must return an error when context is already canceled")
}

func TestStoreClose_RejectsCanceledContext(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	cancel()
	s := newTestStore(t)
	err := s.Close(ctx, "missing")
	require.Error(t, err, "Close must return an error when context is already canceled")
}

func TestExternalBackendCarriesLogicalCollectionName(t *testing.T) {
	toolDir := t.TempDir()
	writeFakeBackendTool(t, toolDir, "bd")
	writeFakeBackendTool(t, toolDir, "br")
	t.Setenv("PATH", toolDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))

	for _, tc := range []struct {
		name       string
		backend    string
		collection string
	}{
		{name: "default-bd", backend: "bd", collection: DefaultCollection},
		{name: "exec-runs-bd", backend: "bd", collection: "exec-runs"},
		{name: "agent-sessions-br", backend: "br", collection: "agent-sessions"},
	} {
		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND", tc.backend)
			s := NewStore(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ddxroot.DirName), WithCollection(tc.collection))
			backend, ok := s.backend.(*ExternalBackend)
			require.True(t, ok)
			assert.Equal(t, tc.backend, backend.Tool)
			assert.Equal(t, tc.collection, backend.Collection)
		})
	}
}

func TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends(t *testing.T) {
	toolDir := t.TempDir()
	writeFakeBackendTool(t, toolDir, "bd")
	writeFakeBackendTool(t, toolDir, "br")
	t.Setenv("PATH", toolDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))

	for _, tc := range []struct {
		name       string
		backend    string
		collection string
	}{
		{name: "bd", backend: "bd", collection: DefaultCollection},
		{name: "br", backend: "br", collection: "exec-runs"},
	} {
		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			tempDir := t.TempDir()
			writeStoreConfig(t, tempDir, tc.backend)
			s := NewStore(filepath.Join(tempDir, ddxroot.DirName), WithCollection(tc.collection))
			backend, ok := s.backend.(*ExternalBackend)
			require.True(t, ok)
			assert.Equal(t, tc.backend, backend.Tool)
			assert.Equal(t, tc.collection, backend.Collection)
		})
	}
}

func TestExternalBackendFallsBackWhenToolMissing(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("PATH", "")

	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ddxroot.DirName), WithCollection("exec-runs"))
	assert.Nil(t, s.backend)
}

	require.True(t, ok, "expected ExternalBackend for bd + non-default collection")

// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
type Store struct {
	Collection string
	Dir        string
	File       string
	Prefix     string
	LockDir    string
	LockWait   time.Duration
	backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
}

// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface

// cli/cmd callers program against.
var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)

type StoreOption func(*Store)

// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
	return func(s *Store) {
		if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
			s.Collection = cleaned
		}
	}
}

// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
	if dir == "" {
		dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	}
	workingDir := dir
	if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
		workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
	}
	prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
	var configBackend string
	if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
		if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
			prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
		}
		configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
	}
	if prefix == "" {
		prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
	}
	// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
	backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
	if backendType == "" {
		backendType = configBackend
	}
	if backendType == "" {
		backendType = BackendJSONL
	}

	s := &Store{
		Collection: DefaultCollection,
		Dir:        dir,
		Prefix:     prefix,
		LockWait:   parseDurationOr("DDX_BEAD_LOCK_TIMEOUT", 10*time.Second),
	}
	for _, opt := range opts {
		if opt != nil {
			opt(s)
		}
	}
	spec := DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
	s.File, s.LockDir = spec.PathsUnder(dir)

	// Set up external backend if configured. bd/br lack per-collection
	// scoping in their CLI, so non-default collections (e.g. beads-archive)
	// route through a JSONL fallback the external backend transparently
	// delegates to. The default collection always goes straight to bd/br
	// to preserve the existing interchange contract (schema_compat_test.go,
	// import/export round-trip).
	switch backendType {
	case BackendBD, BackendBR:
		var fallback RawBackend
		if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
			fallback = NewJSONLBackend(s.Dir, s.File, s.LockDir, s.LockWait)
		}
		if ext, err := NewExternalBackendWithFallback(backendType, s.Collection, fallback); err == nil {
			s.backend = ext
		}
		// Fall through to JSONL if tool not available
	case BackendAxon:
		// Per ddx-95ec5ed5 AC §3 the axon backend ships behind a feature
		// flag until ddx-743bc194 (chaos conformance) signs off. Routing
		// only happens when DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy; otherwise we
		// warn once and fall through to JSONL so the workspace stays
		// usable on misconfiguration.
		if AxonExperimentalEnabled() {
			s.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
		} else {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: backend=axon ignored: set %s=1 to opt in (axon backend is experimental)\n", AxonExperimentalEnv)
		}
	}

	return s
}

// NewStoreWithCollection creates a store for a named logical collection.
func NewStoreWithCollection(dir, collection string) *Store {
	return NewStore(dir, WithCollection(collection))
}

// Init creates the storage directory and file.
func (s *Store) Init() error {
	if s.backend != nil {
		return s.backend.Init()
	}
	if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
	}
	f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("bead: init file: %w", err)
	}
	return f.Close()
}

// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
func (s *Store) GenID() (string, error) {
	b := make([]byte, 4)
	if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("bead: gen id: %w", err)
	}
	return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", s.Prefix, hex.EncodeToString(b)), nil
}

// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
func (s *Store) ReadAll() ([]Bead, error) {
	return s.ReadAllFiltered(nil)
}

// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(pred func(Bead) bool) ([]Bead, error) {
	if s.backend != nil {
		all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		if pred == nil {
			return all, nil
		}
		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
		for _, b := range all {
			if pred(b) {
				out = append(out, b)
			}
		}
		return out, nil
	}
	beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
	}
	beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
	for _, warning := range warnings {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
	}
	if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
		repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
		if repairErr != nil {
			return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
		}
		if repaired {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
		}
	}
	if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
	}
"}}

4	  depends_on:

8	---
9	# Technical Design: Bead-Backed Collection Abstraction
10	
11	## Purpose
12	
13	This design picks the concrete implementation choices needed to land
14	ADR-004 ("Use Bead-Backed Collections for DDx Runtime Storage") on top
15	of the existing single-file `.ddx/beads.jsonl` tracker described in
16	SD-004 and required by FEAT-004.
17	
18	ADR-004 commits DDx to a model of named bead-backed collections with
19	sidecar attachments. SD-004 describes the existing single-file store and
20	its locking, repair, and append-only evidence semantics. FEAT-004 sets
21	the user-visible behavior of the active tracker. This TD specifies:
22	
23	(a) the collection registry shape,
24	(b) the archival trigger policy and parameters,
25	(c) the attachment storage layout,
26	(d) the migration semantics for the existing 5.4 MB `beads.jsonl`,
27	(e) the read-path semantics across active plus archive, and
28	(f) the bd/br interchange compatibility for the archive collection.
29	
30	The decisions below are the defaults DDx will ship. Anything outside
31	this list — for example new collections beyond `beads`, `beads-archive`,
32	`exec-runs`, and `agent-sessions` — is out of scope for this TD and
33	should be handled in a follow-up TD that depends on this one.
34	
35	## (a) Collection Registry
36	
37	### Registry Shape
38	
39	A collection is a named logical store backed by one of the existing
40	bead backends. The registry is an in-process Go map seeded at startup
41	from a small declarative table. It is not a user-editable file; users
42	do not name new collections, and DDx does not auto-discover collections
43	from disk.
44	
45	```go
46	type CollectionID string
47	
48	type CollectionSpec struct {
49	    ID              CollectionID // "beads", "beads-archive", ...
50	    DefaultBackend  BackendKind  // jsonl | bd | br
51	    JSONLPath       string       // path under .ddx/ for the jsonl backend
52	    QueueSemantics  bool         // true for "beads" only
53	    ArchivePartner  CollectionID // "beads-archive" for "beads", "" otherwise
54	    Attachments     bool         // true for collections that allow sidecars
55	}
56	```
57	
58	Concrete shipping registry:
59	
60	| ID               | JSONL path                       | QueueSemantics | ArchivePartner | Attachments |
61	|------------------|----------------------------------|----------------|----------------|-------------|
62	| `beads`          | `.ddx/beads.jsonl`               | yes            | `beads-archive`| no          |
63	| `beads-archive`  | `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`       | no             | (none)         | no          |
64	| `exec-runs`      | `.ddx/exec-runs.jsonl`           | no             | (none)         | yes         |
65	| `agent-sessions` | `.ddx/agent-sessions.jsonl`      | no             | (none)         | yes         |
66	
67	### Store API
68	
69	The current `beads.Store` API is generalized so the file path is no
70	longer hardcoded. The minimum surface is:
71	
72	```go
73	type Store interface {
74	    Collection(id CollectionID) Collection
75	}
76	
77	type Collection interface {
78	    List(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
79	    Get(ctx context.Context, id string) (Bead, error)
80	    Append(ctx context.Context, b Bead) error
81	    Update(ctx context.Context, b Bead) error
82	    Delete(ctx context.Context, id string) error
83	    Lock(ctx context.Context) (Unlocker, error)
84	}
85	```
86	
87	`Lock` is per-collection so that archiving from `beads` to
88	`beads-archive` can hold both locks in a fixed order (`beads` first,
89	then `beads-archive`) to avoid deadlocks.
90	
91	The existing locking, atomic temp-file rename, malformed-record repair,
92	and `Extra` preservation behaviors from SD-004 carry over unchanged on
93	a per-collection basis. Each JSONL collection has its own `.lock`,
94	`.tmp`, and `.bak` files alongside its primary file.
95	
96	### Backend Selection
97	
98	`bd` and `br` backends already accept a collection name through their
99	own CLIs. The DDx external-backend adapter passes the collection ID
100	through to those CLIs. For backends that do not support multiple named
101	collections, the adapter returns a clear error when a non-default
102	collection is requested rather than silently merging records.
103	
104	## (b) Archival Trigger Policy
105	
106	### Goal
107	
108	Keep the active queue small enough that `ddx bead list` and the SD-004
109	read path stay under the 100 ms / 10,000 bead target stated in SD-004,
110	without forcing operators to think about archival.
111	
112	### Trigger
113	
114	Archival runs on demand through `ddx bead archive` and opportunistically
115	after any close-causing mutation when the active collection exceeds a
116	threshold. There is no background daemon.
117	
118	### Defaults
119	
120	| Parameter                      | Default                                |
121	|--------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
122	| `archive.enabled`              | `true`                                 |
123	| `archive.min_age`              | `30d` since `closed_at`                |
124	| `archive.min_active_count`     | `2000` records in `beads`              |
125	| `archive.batch_size`           | `500` per opportunistic pass           |
126	| `archive.statuses`             | `closed`, `wont_fix`, `superseded`     |
127	| `archive.opportunistic`        | `true` (run after close mutations)     |
128	| `archive.preserve_dependencies`| `true` (keep edges resolvable)         |
129	
130	A bead is eligible to archive when its `status` is in
131	`archive.statuses`, its `closed_at` (or `updated_at` fallback) is older
132	than `archive.min_age`, and no open bead in `beads` lists it as a
133	dependency. The dependency check prevents archiving a closed bead that
134	an open bead still references; archived beads remain readable, but
135	queue derivation in SD-004 should not have to load `beads-archive` to
136	decide `ready`/`blocked`.
137	
138	### Configuration
139	
140	Defaults live in `config.go` and may be overridden through `.ddx.yml`:
141	
142	```yaml
143	archive:
144	  enabled: true
145	  min_age: 30d
146	  min_active_count: 2000
147	  batch_size: 500
148	  statuses: [closed, wont_fix, superseded]
149	  opportunistic: true
150	```
151	
152	### Mutation Sequence
153	
154	1. Acquire `beads` lock.
155	2. Acquire `beads-archive` lock.
156	3. Read both snapshots.
157	4. Select up to `batch_size` eligible records from `beads`.
158	5. Append the selected records to `beads-archive` with an added
159	   `archived_at` timestamp in `Extra`.
160	6. Remove the selected records from `beads`.
161	7. Atomic temp-file rename for `beads-archive` first, then for `beads`.
162	   This ordering means a crash mid-archive leaves a record in both
163	   collections rather than nowhere, and the read path in (e) hides the
164	   duplicate behind active-wins precedence.
165	8. Release locks in reverse order.
166	
167	## (c) Attachment Storage Layout
168	
169	### Layout
170	
171	Attachments are sidecar files under a per-collection directory keyed by
172	record ID:
173	
174	```
175	.ddx/attachments/
176	  <collection-id>/
177	    <record-id>/
178	      events.jsonl
179	      prompt.txt
180	      response.txt
181	      stdout.log
182	      <name>.<ext>
183	```
184	
185	Concrete defaults:
186	
187	- The active `beads` collection does not use sidecar attachments.
188	  Evidence stays in `Extra["events"]` per SD-004, because that history
189	  is small and is referenced often by queue tooling.
190	- `exec-runs` and `agent-sessions` use sidecars by default for prompt,
191	  response, stdout, stderr, and structured result blobs.
192	- `beads-archive` does not introduce new attachments. Records moved
193	  from `beads` carry their existing `Extra["events"]` history inline.
194	
195	### Reference Format
196	
197	Attachment references are stored under a reserved
198	`Extra["attachments"]` array on the record:
199	
200	```json
201	{
202	  "attachments": [
203	    {
204	      "name": "prompt",
205	      "path": "exec-runs/run-2026-05-01-abc/prompt.txt",
206	      "media_type": "text/plain",
207	      "sha256": "…",
208	      "size": 12345
209	    }
210	  ]
211	}
212	```
213	
214	`path` is repository-relative under `.ddx/attachments/`. `sha256` and
215	`size` are recorded at write time and verified on read. The reference
216	key `attachments` is DDx-specific and lives in preserved extras as
217	required by ADR-004; it does not rename or shadow any bd/br field.
218	
219	### Write Algorithm
220	
221	1. Write the attachment to a temp path under
222	   `.ddx/attachments/<collection>/<id>/.<name>.tmp`.
223	2. `fsync`, then rename to the final path.
224	3. Compute and record `sha256` and `size`.
225	4. Append the reference to `Extra["attachments"]`.
226	5. Persist the record through the normal collection write path so the
227	   reference becomes durable atomically with the temp-file rename of
228	   the collection JSONL file.
229	
230	### Garbage Collection
231	
232	Attachments are removed only when their owning record is removed. A
233	`ddx bead gc` command (out of scope for this TD beyond the contract)
234	walks `.ddx/attachments/<collection>/` and deletes directories whose
235	record IDs no longer exist in the collection. There is no time-based
236	attachment expiration.
237	
238	## (d) Migration Plan for Existing `beads.jsonl`
239	
240	The current `.ddx/beads.jsonl` is 5.4 MB and 1,172 lines. The
241	migration must be safe to run on an in-place repository checkout.
242	
243	### Steps
244	
245	1. **Compatibility shim first.** The new collection registry treats
246	   `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as the JSONL path for the `beads` collection. No
247	   file move is required to keep the active tracker working. Existing
248	   readers and writers continue to operate against the same path.
249	
250	2. **Idempotent create-on-write for archive.** The
251	   `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` file is created lazily on the first
252	   archive operation. Absence of the file is treated as an empty
253	   archive collection.
254	
255	3. **One-shot backfill command.** `ddx bead migrate-archive` performs
256	   the initial archival pass:
257	   - Acquire both locks.
258	   - Make a backup copy of `.ddx/beads.jsonl` to
259	     `.ddx/beads.jsonl.pre-archive.bak`.
260	   - Move all records that match the archival policy in (b) using the
261	     normal mutation sequence.
262	   - Print a summary of moved/retained counts.
263	   This command is opt-in. Users who never run it keep the current
264	   single-file behavior.
265	
266	4. **No schema rewrite.** Existing records are not rewritten to add
267	   new fields. The `attachments` extra appears only on new
268	   `exec-runs` and `agent-sessions` records.
269	
270	5. **Rollback.** `ddx bead migrate-archive --rollback` re-merges
271	   `beads-archive.jsonl` into `beads.jsonl` under both locks, atomic
272	   temp-file rename, and removes the archive file when empty.
273	
274	6. **Doc audit.** The migration command is documented in the bead
275	   command reference. `ddx doc audit` should pass after this TD lands
276	   because the new TD declares its dependencies and no existing artifact
277	   needs to change its `depends_on`.
278	
279	### Risk Mitigation
280	
281	- The default `archive.min_active_count` of 2000 means existing
282	  installations under that count will not be archived opportunistically
283	  on close. They keep behaving like today's single-file tracker until
284	  they cross the threshold or run `ddx bead migrate-archive`.
285	- The pre-archive backup file is never removed automatically.
286	
287	## (e) Read-Path Semantics Across Active and Archive
288	
289	### Default View
290	
291	Queue commands defined by SD-004 (`list`, `ready`, `blocked`, `status`)
292	read only the `beads` collection. They do not load `beads-archive`.
293	This preserves the SD-004 100 ms target as the active set stays bounded
294	and matches user expectation that "what's on the queue" is the active
295	queue.
296	
297	### Merged View
298	
299	`ddx bead show <id>`, `ddx bead history`, and any explicit
300	`--include-archive` flag use a merged view:
301	
302	1. Look up the bead in `beads` first.
303	2. Fall back to `beads-archive` if not found.
304	3. For listing operations with `--include-archive`, lazily concatenate
305	   the two snapshots and de-duplicate by `id` with active-wins
306	   precedence (so a record present in both collections after an
307	   interrupted archive is reported as the active copy).
308	
309	### Lazy Loading
310	
311	The archive collection is opened only when a merged view is requested.
312	The store keeps per-collection snapshots cached for the lifetime of a
313	single command invocation; commands do not share snapshots across
314	invocations.
315	
316	### Deletion Semantics
317	
318	`ddx bead delete <id>` removes from the collection that currently holds
319	the record. If the record is in both collections (post-crash), the
320	delete removes it from both under both locks before returning success.
321	
322	### Dependency Resolution
323	
324	When the queue derivation in SD-004 needs to display a closed
325	dependency that has been archived, it reads only the dependency's
326	`status` from `beads-archive` on demand and caches it in memory for the
327	remainder of the command. It never promotes archived beads back into
328	the active snapshot.
329	
330	## (f) bd/br Interchange Compatibility for the Archive Collection
331	
332	### Constraints from ADR-004
333	
334	ADR-004 requires that bd/br interchange continue to work through
335	`list --json` for reads and `import --from jsonl --replace -` for
336	writes, and that the bead envelope keeps its shared field names.
337	
338	### Decision
339	
340	The archive collection participates in interchange on the same terms
341	as the active collection.
342	
343	- `ddx bead export --collection beads-archive` emits one bead-record
344	  JSON object per line, identical schema to active beads.
345	- `ddx bead import --collection beads-archive --from jsonl -` accepts
346	  the same shape.
347	- `archived_at` is stored in `Extra` and round-trips as an unknown
348	  field for bd/br, exactly like other DDx-specific fields. It is not
349	  promoted to a top-level field.
350	- The `attachments` extra also round-trips. bd/br do not interpret it,
351	  but they do not strip preserved extras, so references survive an
352	  export/import cycle.
353	
354	### Adapter Behavior
355	
356	For backends that support multiple named collections, the DDx adapter
357	passes the collection ID through. For backends that do not, the
358	adapter returns an error when the user asks for a non-default
359	collection rather than silently merging records into the default
360	collection. This is the same rule as (a).
361	
362	### Schema Compatibility Test
363	
364	`schema_compat_test.go` is extended with an "archive round-trip" case:
365	take a record from the active collection, archive it, export it,
366	re-import it into a fresh `beads-archive` collection, and verify
367	field-for-field equality including preserved extras.
368	
369	## Validation
370	
371	These checks must pass after this TD is implemented:
372	
373	- `go test ./internal/bead/...`
374	- `go test ./cmd -run 'TestBeadCollection|TestArchive|TestAttachment' -count=1`
375	- `ddx doc audit` shows TD-027 with no broken edges.
376	- Manual: run `ddx bead migrate-archive` on a copy of an existing
377	  repository checkout and confirm queue commands produce the same
378	  active snapshot they did before migration.
379	
380	## Non-Goals
381	
382	- Adding collections beyond `beads`, `beads-archive`, `exec-runs`, and
383	  `agent-sessions`. Future collections require a follow-up TD.
384	- Real-time replication between collections.
385	- Cross-repository archive sharing.
386	- Background or scheduled archival; archival only runs on user-initiated
387	  commands or as an opportunistic step inside an already-mutating
388	  command.
389	"}}

9	// CollectionID is the logical name of a bead-backed collection.
10	// "beads" is the active queue; future collections (e.g. "beads-archive",
11	// "exec-runs", "agent-sessions") share the same on-disk format and store
12	// API but live under their own files and locks.
13	type CollectionID string
14	
15	// CollectionSpec is the shipping description of one collection. It maps a
16	// logical name to the concrete on-disk artifacts the JSONL backend uses.
17	//
18	// Per TD-027 §(a), the registry is an in-process table seeded at startup;
19	// it is not user-editable and DDx does not auto-discover collections from
20	// disk. Additional fields from TD-027 (QueueSemantics, ArchivePartner,
21	// Attachments) are deliberately omitted at this stage — TD-027 C3 will
22	// introduce them when archive logic lands. This bead (C2) is scoped to the
23	// storage-engine refactor: registry shape plus path/lock decoupling.
24	type CollectionSpec struct {
25		// ID is the logical collection name, e.g. "beads".
26		ID CollectionID
27		// JSONLFile is the file name (not a full path) under the .ddx
28		// directory that the JSONL backend reads and writes. Empty means
29		// "<id>.jsonl".
30		JSONLFile string
31		// LockDirName is the lock directory name under the .ddx directory.
32		// Empty means "<id>.lock".
33		LockDirName string
34	}
35	
36	// FileName returns the JSONL filename for this collection, falling back to
37	// the conventional "<id>.jsonl" form when JSONLFile is unset.
38	func (c CollectionSpec) FileName() string {
39		if c.JSONLFile != "" {
40			return c.JSONLFile
41		}
42		return string(c.ID) + ".jsonl"
43	}
44	
45	// LockName returns the lock-directory name for this collection, falling
46	// back to the conventional "<id>.lock" form when LockDirName is unset.
47	func (c CollectionSpec) LockName() string {
48		if c.LockDirName != "" {
49			return c.LockDirName
50		}
51		return string(c.ID) + ".lock"
52	}
53	
54	// PathsUnder returns the absolute file and lock paths for this collection
55	// rooted at the given .ddx directory.
56	func (c CollectionSpec) PathsUnder(ddxDir string) (file, lockDir string) {
57		return filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.FileName()), filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.LockName())
58	}
59	
60	// Registry is a table of known collection specs keyed by ID. Lookups for
61	// IDs that are not registered are not an error: NewStore synthesizes a
62	// conventional spec on the fly so existing call sites that use ad-hoc
63	// collection names (e.g. test fixtures) keep working. The registry is the
64	// authoritative source for the shipping collections that DDx itself
65	// instantiates.
66	type Registry struct {
67		mu    sync.RWMutex
68		specs map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec
69	}
70	
71	// NewRegistry returns an empty registry.
72	func NewRegistry() *Registry {
73		return &Registry{specs: make(map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec)}
74	}
75	
76	// Register adds or replaces a spec.
77	func (r *Registry) Register(spec CollectionSpec) {
78		r.mu.Lock()
79		defer r.mu.Unlock()
80		r.specs[spec.ID] = spec
81	}
82	
83	// Lookup returns the spec for id and whether it was registered. Callers
84	// that want a usable spec for any name should prefer Resolve.
85	func (r *Registry) Lookup(id CollectionID) (CollectionSpec, bool) {
86		r.mu.RLock()
87		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
88		spec, ok := r.specs[id]
89		return spec, ok
90	}
91	
92	// Resolve returns the spec for id, synthesizing a conventional one (with
93	// "<id>.jsonl" / "<id>.lock") when id is not registered. This keeps the
94	// JSONL backend's per-collection layout uniform whether or not a name is
95	// in the shipping table.
96	func (r *Registry) Resolve(id CollectionID) CollectionSpec {
97		if spec, ok := r.Lookup(id); ok {
98			return spec
99		}
100		return CollectionSpec{ID: id}
101	}
102	
103	// IDs returns the registered collection IDs in lexical order.
104	func (r *Registry) IDs() []CollectionID {
105		r.mu.RLock()
106		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
107		out := make([]CollectionID, 0, len(r.specs))
108		for id := range r.specs {
109			out = append(out, id)
110		}
111		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i] < out[j] })
112		return out
113	}
114	
115	// defaultRegistry is the process-wide registry. It ships with the active
116	// "beads" collection and its archive partner "beads-archive" (TD-027 §a).
117	// Additional collections (exec-runs, agent-sessions) will be registered by
118	// their owning beads.
119	var defaultRegistry = func() *Registry {
120		r := NewRegistry()
121		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
122			ID:          DefaultCollection,
123			JSONLFile:   DefaultCollection + ".jsonl",
124			LockDirName: DefaultCollection + ".lock",
125		})
126		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
127			ID:          BeadsArchiveCollection,
128			JSONLFile:   BeadsArchiveCollection + ".jsonl",
129			LockDirName: BeadsArchiveCollection + ".lock",
130		})
131		return r
132	}()
133	
134	// DefaultRegistry returns the process-wide collection registry.
135	func DefaultRegistry() *Registry {
136		return defaultRegistry
137	}

145	// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
146	func (s *Store) ReadAll() ([]Bead, error) {
147		return s.ReadAllFiltered(nil)

150	// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
151	// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
152	// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
153	// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
154	// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
155	// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
156	// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
157	func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(pred func(Bead) bool) ([]Bead, error) {
158		if s.backend != nil {
159			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
160			if err != nil {
161				return nil, err
162			}
163			if pred == nil {
164				return all, nil
165			}
166			out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
167			for _, b := range all {
168				if pred(b) {
169					out = append(out, b)
170				}
171			}
172			return out, nil
173		}
174		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
175		if err != nil {
176			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
177		}
178		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
179		for _, warning := range warnings {
180			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
181		}
182		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
183			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
184			if repairErr != nil {
185				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
186			}
187			if repaired {
188				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
189			}
190		}
191		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
192			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
193		}
194		if pred == nil {
195			return beads, nil
196		}
197		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(beads))
198		for _, b := range beads {
199			if pred(b) {
200				out = append(out, b)
201			}
202		}
203		return out, nil
204	}
205	
206	func (s *Store) readAllRaw() ([]Bead, []string, error) {
207		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
208		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
209			return nil, nil, nil
210		}
211		if err != nil {
212			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("read: %w", err)
213		}
214		beads, warnings, err := parseBeadJSONL(data)
215		if err != nil {
216			return nil, nil, err
217		}
218		return beads, warnings, nil
219	}
220	
221	func parseBeadJSONL(data []byte) ([]Bead, []string, error) {
222		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(data))
223		// 16MB max line. Real-world extremes observed: 1.46MB bead rows when a
224		// reviewer stream dumped into events[].body, and bd-exported lines stacking
225		// many 65KB fields. bd's upstream per-field cap is 65,535 bytes; an
226		// individual bead with dozens of maxed-out fields fits comfortably in 16MB.
227		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
228	
229		var beads []Bead
230		var warnings []string
231		lineNo := 0
232		for scanner.Scan() {
233			lineNo++
234			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
235			if line == "" {
236				continue
237			}
238			b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(line))
239			if err != nil {
240				warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf("bead: read line %d: %v", lineNo, err))
241				continue
242			}
243			beads = append(beads, b)
244		}
245		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
246			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("scan jsonl: %w", err)
247		}
248		return beads, warnings, nil
249	}
250	
251	func (s *Store) repairJSONL() (bool, error) {
252		var repaired bool
253		err := s.WithLock(func() error {
254			beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
255			if err != nil {
256				return err
257			}
258			beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
259			if len(warnings) == 0 || len(beads) == 0 {
260				return nil
261			}
262			backupPath := s.File + ".bak"
263			backupData, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
264			if err != nil {
265				return fmt.Errorf("read current file: %w", err)
266			}
267			if err := writeAtomicFile(backupPath, backupData); err != nil {
268				return fmt.Errorf("write backup: %w", err)
269			}
270			if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
271				return fmt.Errorf("write repaired file: %w", err)
272			}
273			repaired = true
274			return nil
275		})
276		return repaired, err
277	}
278	
279	func foldLatestBeads(beads []Bead) []Bead {
280		if len(beads) == 0 {
281			return nil
282		}
283	
284		latest := make(map[string]Bead, len(beads))
285		lastSeen := make(map[string]int, len(beads))
286		for i, b := range beads {
287			latest[b.ID] = b
288			lastSeen[b.ID] = i
289		}
290	
291		ids := make([]string, 0, len(latest))
292		for id := range latest {
293			ids = append(ids, id)
294		}
295		sort.Slice(ids, func(i, j int) bool {
296			if lastSeen[ids[i]] != lastSeen[ids[j]] {
297				return lastSeen[ids[i]] < lastSeen[ids[j]]

func (s *Store) Close(id string) error {
	if err := s.Update(id, func(b *Bead) {
		b.Status = StatusClosed
	}); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	s.maybeOpportunisticArchive()
	return nil
}","new_string":"// Close sets a bead's status to closed. When the close succeeds and the bead
// carries an inline event history, those events are moved to a sidecar
// attachment under .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl so the active row stays
// small (per ADR-004's attachment model and TD-027 §c).
func (s *Store) Close(id string) error {
	if err := s.Update(id, func(b *Bead) {
		b.Status = StatusClosed
	}); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if err := s.externalizeEvents(id); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	s.maybeOpportunisticArchive()
	return nil
}

// externalizeEvents moves a bead's inline events into its sidecar attachment
// under a fresh lock. Safe to call repeatedly — no-op when there are no
// inline events (already externalized, or none recorded).
func (s *Store) externalizeEvents(id string) error {
	return s.WithLock(func() error {
		beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		for i := range beads {
			if beads[i].ID != id {
				continue
			}
			if err := s.externalizeEventsInPlace(&beads[i]); err != nil {
				return err
			}
			return s.WriteAll(beads)
		}
		return nil
	})
}"}}}

.ddx/executions/20260506T092212-36d077c0/embedded/agent-codex-1778059339179612114.jsonl:3:{"type":"tool_result","sequence":2,"time":"2026-05-06T09:22:25.557606827Z","metadata":{"attempt_id":"20260506T092212-36d077c0","base_rev":"521e1c806a51bb800d0baeba067aa49417f912c7","bead_id":"ddx-29f02cf4","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260506T092212-36d077c0","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260506T092212-36d077c0/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"cb6ca51c5579fdd770a7842edc0dbe6c57e4136c077ee0f3f60ca581e6b0b0f9","session_id":"eb-487526f6","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_1","output":"// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
//
// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
//
// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
// reference in the event body.
//
// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
// matches the scanner in the agent package.
package bead

import (
	"bufio"
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"sort"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
)

// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
type Store struct {
	Collection string
	Dir        string
	File       string
	Prefix     string
	LockDir    string
	LockWait   time.Duration
	backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
}

// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
// cli/cmd callers program against.
var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)

type StoreOption func(*Store)

// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
	return func(s *Store) {
		if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
			s.Collection = cleaned
		}
	}
}

// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
	if dir == "" {
		dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	}
	workingDir := dir
	if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
		workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
	}
	prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
	var configBackend string
	if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
		if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
			prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
		}
		configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
	}
	if prefix == "" {
		prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
	}
	// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
	backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
	if backendType == "" {
		backendType = configBackend
	}
	if backendType == "" {
		backendType = BackendJSONL
	}

	s := &Store{
		Collection: DefaultCollection,
		Dir:        dir,
		Prefix:     prefix,
		LockWait:   parseDurationOr("DDX_BEAD_LOCK_TIMEOUT", 10*time.Second),
	}
	for _, opt := range opts {
		if opt != nil {
			opt(s)
		}
	}
	spec := DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
	s.File, s.LockDir = spec.PathsUnder(dir)

	// Set up external backend if configured. bd/br lack per-collection
	// scoping in their CLI, so non-default collections (e.g. beads-archive)
	// route through a JSONL fallback the external backend transparently
	// delegates to. The default collection always goes straight to bd/br
	// to preserve the existing interchange contract (schema_compat_test.go,
	// import/export round-trip).
	switch backendType {
	case BackendBD, BackendBR:
		var fallback RawBackend
		if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
			fallback = NewJSONLBackend(s.Dir, s.File, s.LockDir, s.LockWait)
		}
		if ext, err := NewExternalBackendWithFallback(backendType, s.Collection, fallback); err == nil {
			s.backend = ext
		}
		// Fall through to JSONL if tool not available
	case BackendAxon:
		// Per ddx-95ec5ed5 AC §3 the axon backend ships behind a feature
		// flag until ddx-743bc194 (chaos conformance) signs off. Routing
		// only happens when DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy; otherwise we
		// warn once and fall through to JSONL so the workspace stays
		// usable on misconfiguration.
		if AxonExperimentalEnabled() {
			s.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
		} else {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: backend=axon ignored: set %s=1 to opt in (axon backend is experimental)\n", AxonExperimentalEnv)
		}
	}

	return s
}

// NewStoreWithCollection creates a store for a named logical collection.
func NewStoreWithCollection(dir, collection string) *Store {
	return NewStore(dir, WithCollection(collection))
}

// Init creates the storage directory and file.
func (s *Store) Init() error {
	if s.backend != nil {
		return s.backend.Init()
	}
	if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
	}
	f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("bead: init file: %w", err)
	}
	return f.Close()
}

// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
func (s *Store) GenID() (string, error) {
	b := make([]byte, 4)
	if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("bead: gen id: %w", err)
	}
	return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", s.Prefix, hex.EncodeToString(b)), nil
}

// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
func (s *Store) ReadAll() ([]Bead, error) {
	return s.ReadAllFiltered(nil)
}

// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(pred func(Bead) bool) ([]Bead, error) {
	if s.backend != nil {
		all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		if pred == nil {
			return all, nil
		}
		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
		for _, b := range all {
			if pred(b) {
				out = append(out, b)
			}
		}
		return out, nil
	}
	beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
	}
	beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
	for _, warning := range warnings {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
	}
	if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
		repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
		if repairErr != nil {
			return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
		}
		if repaired {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
		}
	}
	if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
	}

.ddx/executions/20260503T223141-dbbc3885/embedded/agent-claude-1777847507555130551.jsonl:10:{"type":"tool_result","sequence":9,"time":"2026-05-03T22:33:00.317700617Z","metadata":{"attempt_id":"20260503T223141-dbbc3885","base_rev":"58e955ce146ea309a9f15fe4ca89d7aa815be562","bead_id":"ddx-743bc194","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260503T223141-dbbc3885","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260503T223141-dbbc3885/prompt.md","session_id":"eb-2b651b2a","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"toolu_01UFYgJ9mTA4JzhmYnFB6wGg","output":"1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.
18	package bead
19	
20	import (
21		"bufio"
22		"bytes"
23		"context"
24		"crypto/rand"
25		"encoding/hex"
26		"encoding/json"
27		"fmt"
28		"os"
29		"path/filepath"
30		"sort"
31		"strconv"
32		"strings"
33		"time"
34	
35		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
36		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
37	)
38	
39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
51	// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
52	// cli/cmd callers program against.
53	var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)
54	
55	type StoreOption func(*Store)
56	
57	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
58	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
59	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
60		return func(s *Store) {
61			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
62				s.Collection = cleaned
63			}
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
68	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
69	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
70		if dir == "" {
71			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
72		}
73		workingDir := dir
74		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
75			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
76		}
77		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
78		var configBackend string
79		if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
80			if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
81				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
82			}
83			configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
84		}
85		if prefix == "" {
86			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
87		}
88		// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
89		backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
90		if backendType == "" {
91			backendType = configBackend
92		}
93		if backendType == "" {
94			backendType = BackendJSONL
95		}
96	
97		s := &Store{
98			Collection: DefaultCollection,
99			Dir:        dir,
100			Prefix:     prefix,
101			LockWait:   parseDurationOr("DDX_BEAD_LOCK_TIMEOUT", 10*time.Second),
102		}
103		for _, opt := range opts {
104			if opt != nil {
105				opt(s)
106			}
107		}
108		spec := DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
109		s.File, s.LockDir = spec.PathsUnder(dir)
110	
111		// Set up external backend if configured. bd/br lack per-collection
112		// scoping in their CLI, so non-default collections (e.g. beads-archive)
113		// route through a JSONL fallback the external backend transparently
114		// delegates to. The default collection always goes straight to bd/br
115		// to preserve the existing interchange contract (schema_compat_test.go,
116		// import/export round-trip).
117		switch backendType {
118		case BackendBD, BackendBR:
119			var fallback RawBackend
120			if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
121				fallback = NewJSONLBackend(s.Dir, s.File, s.LockDir, s.LockWait)
122			}
123			if ext, err := NewExternalBackendWithFallback(backendType, s.Collection, fallback); err == nil {
124				s.backend = ext
125			}
126			// Fall through to JSONL if tool not available
127		case BackendAxon:
128			// Per ddx-95ec5ed5 AC §3 the axon backend ships behind a feature
129			// flag until ddx-743bc194 (chaos conformance) signs off. Routing
130			// only happens when DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy; otherwise we
131			// warn once and fall through to JSONL so the workspace stays
132			// usable on misconfiguration.
133			if AxonExperimentalEnabled() {
134				s.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
135			} else {
136				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: backend=axon ignored: set %s=1 to opt in (axon backend is experimental)\n", AxonExperimentalEnv)
137			}
138		}
139	
140		return s
141	}

155	// Init creates the storage directory and file.
156	func (s *Store) Init() error {

158			return s.backend.Init()
159		}

161			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
162		}
163		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)

170	// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
171	func (s *Store) GenID() (string, error) {
172		b := make([]byte, 4)
173		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
174			return "", fmt.Errorf("bead: gen id: %w", err)
175		}
176		return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", s.Prefix, hex.EncodeToString(b)), nil

178	
179	// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
180	func (s *Store) ReadAll() ([]Bead, error) {
181		return s.ReadAllFiltered(nil)
182	}
183	
184	// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
185	// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
186	// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
187	// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
188	// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
189	// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
190	// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
191	func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(pred func(Bead) bool) ([]Bead, error) {
192		if s.backend != nil {
193			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
194			if err != nil {
195				return nil, err
196			}
197			if pred == nil {
198				return all, nil
199			}
200			out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
201			for _, b := range all {
202				if pred(b) {
203					out = append(out, b)
204				}
205			}
206			return out, nil
207		}
208		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
209		if err != nil {
210			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
211		}
212		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
213		for _, warning := range warnings {
214			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
215		}
216		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
217			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
218			if repairErr != nil {
219				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
220			}
221			if repaired {
222				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
223			}
224		}
225		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
226			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
227		}
228		if pred == nil {
229			return beads, nil
230		}
231		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(beads))
232		for _, b := range beads {
233			if pred(b) {
234				out = append(out, b)
235			}
236		}
237		return out, nil
238	}
239	
240	func (s *Store) readAllRaw() ([]Bead, []string, error) {
241		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
242		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
243			return nil, nil, nil
244		}
245		if err != nil {
246			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("read: %w", err)
247		}
248		beads, warnings, err := parseBeadJSONL(data)
249		if err != nil {
250			return nil, nil, err
251		}
252		return beads, warnings, nil
253	}
254	
255	func parseBeadJSONL(data []byte) ([]Bead, []string, error) {
256		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(data))
257		// 16MB max line. Real-world extremes observed: 1.46MB bead rows when a
258		// reviewer stream dumped into events[].body, and bd-exported lines stacking
259		// many 65KB fields. bd's upstream per-field cap is 65,535 bytes; an
260		// individual bead with dozens of maxed-out fields fits comfortably in 16MB.
261		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
262	
263		var beads []Bead
264		var warnings []string
265		lineNo := 0
266		for scanner.Scan() {
267			lineNo++
268			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
269			if line == "" {
270				continue
271			}
272			b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(line))
273			if err != nil {
274				warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf("bead: read line %d: %v", lineNo, err))
275				continue
276			}
277			beads = append(beads, b)
278		}
279		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
280			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("scan jsonl: %w", err)
281		}
282		return beads, warnings, nil
283	}
284	
285	func (s *Store) repairJSONL() (bool, error) {
286		var repaired bool
287		err := s.WithLock(func() error {
288			beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
289			if err != nil {
290				return err
291			}
292			beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
293			if len(warnings) == 0 || len(beads) == 0 {
294				return nil
295			}
296			backupPath := s.File + ".bak"
297			backupData, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
298			if err != nil {
299				return fmt.Errorf("read current file: %w", err)
300			}
301			if err := writeAtomicFile(backupPath, backupData); err != nil {
302				return fmt.Errorf("write backup: %w", err)
303			}
304			if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
305				return fmt.Errorf("write repaired file: %w", err)
306			}
307			repaired = true
308			return nil
309		})
310		return repaired, err
311	}
312	
313	func foldLatestBeads(beads []Bead) []Bead {
314		if len(beads) == 0 {
315			return nil
316		}
317	
318		latest := make(map[string]Bead, len(beads))
319		lastSeen := make(map[string]int, len(beads))
320		for i, b := range beads {
321			latest[b.ID] = b
322			lastSeen[b.ID] = i
323		}
324	
325		ids := make([]string, 0, len(latest))
326		for id := range latest {
327			ids = append(ids, id)
328		}
329		sort.Slice(ids, func(i, j int) bool {
330			if lastSeen[ids[i]] != lastSeen[ids[j]] {
331				return lastSeen[ids[i]] < lastSeen[ids[j]]
332			}
333			return ids[i] < ids[j]
334		})
335	
336		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(ids))
337		for _, id := range ids {
338			out = append(out, latest[id])
339		}
340		return out
341	}
342	
343	func (s *Store) readAllLatestRaw() ([]Bead, []string, error) {
344		// When a non-JSONL backend is configured, the raw bead corpus lives
345		// outside .ddx/<collection>.jsonl entirely (axon: two collections under
346		// .ddx/axon/; bd/br: an external tool's store). Delegate the read so
347		// every read-modify-write path inside Store stays consistent with the
348		// backend's storage layout. Backends are responsible for returning
349		// already-folded beads.
350		if s.backend != nil {
351			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
352			if err != nil {
353				return nil, nil, err
354			}
355			return all, nil, nil
356		}
357		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
358		if err != nil {
359			return nil, nil, err
360		}
361		return foldLatestBeads(beads), warnings, nil
362	}
363	
364	// tmpPath returns a unique temp file path in the same directory as path.
365	// Uses pid + 4 random bytes so concurrent processes don't collide.
366	func tmpPath(path string) (string, error) {
367		b := make([]byte, 4)
368		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
369			return "", err
370		}
371		return fmt.Sprintf("%s.tmp-%d-%s", path, os.Getpid(), hex.EncodeToString(b)), nil
372	}
373	
374	func writeAtomicFile(path string, data []byte) error {
375		tmp, err := tmpPath(path)
376		if err != nil {
377			return err
378		}
379		f, err := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_WRONLY|os.O_CREATE|os.O_EXCL, 0o644)
380		if err != nil {
381			return err
382		}
383		if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
384			f.Close()
385			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
386			return err
387		}
388		if err := f.Sync(); err != nil {
389			f.Close()
390			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
391			return err
392		}
393		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
394			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
395			return err
396		}
397		if err := os.Rename(tmp, path); err != nil {
398			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
399			return err
400		}
401		return nil
402	}
403	
404	func normalizeCollection(name string) string {
405		name = strings.TrimSpace(name)
406		if name == "" {
407			return DefaultCollection
408		}
409		name = strings.TrimSuffix(name, ".jsonl")
410		return name
411	}
412	
413	// WriteAll writes all beads to the configured backend, sorted by ID.
414	func (s *Store) WriteAll(beads []Bead) error {
415		sort.Slice(beads, func(i, j int) bool {
416			return beads[i].ID < beads[j].ID
417		})
418	
419		if s.backend != nil {
420			return s.backend.WriteAll(beads)
421		}
422	
423		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
424			return fmt.Errorf("bead: mkdir: %w", err)
425		}
426	
427		tmp, err := tmpPath(s.File)
428		if err != nil {
429			return fmt.Errorf("bead: tmp name: %w", err)
430		}
431		f, err := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_WRONLY|os.O_CREATE|os.O_EXCL, 0o644)
432		if err != nil {
433			return fmt.Errorf("bead: create tmp: %w", err)
434		}
435	
436		enc := json.NewEncoder(f)
437		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
438		for _, b := range beads {
439			data, err := marshalBead(b)
440			if err != nil {
441				f.Close()
442				os.Remove(tmp)
443				return fmt.Errorf("bead: marshal: %w", err)
444			}
445			if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
446				f.Close()
447				os.Remove(tmp)
448				return fmt.Errorf("bead: write: %w", err)
449			}
450			if _, err := f.WriteString("\n"); err != nil {
451				f.Close()
452				os.Remove(tmp)
453				return fmt.Errorf("bead: write newline: %w", err)
454			}
455		}
456	
457		if err := f.Sync(); err != nil {
458			f.Close()
459			os.Remove(tmp)
460			return fmt.Errorf("bead: sync tmp: %w", err)
461		}
462		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
463			os.Remove(tmp)
464			return fmt.Errorf("bead: close tmp: %w", err)
465		}
466		if err := os.Rename(tmp, s.File); err != nil {
467			os.Remove(tmp)
468			return fmt.Errorf("bead: rename tmp: %w", err)
469		}
470		return nil
471	}
472	
473	// Create adds a new bead. Assigns defaults, validates, then persists.
474	func (s *Store) Create(b *Bead) error {
475		now := time.Now().UTC()
476		if b.ID == "" {
477			id, err := s.GenID()
478			if err != nil {
479				return err
480			}
481			b.ID = id
482		}
483		if b.IssueType == "" {
484			b.IssueType = DefaultType
485		}
486		if b.Status == "" {
487			b.Status = DefaultStatus
488		}
489		b.CreatedAt = now
490		b.UpdatedAt = now
491	
492		// Validate after defaults are applied so hooks see final state
493		if err := s.validateBead(b); err != nil {
494			return err
495		}
496		// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create another
497		// operator-prompt bead. The execute-bead harness exports the actor's
498		// issue_type via DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE; absent the env, the guard is a
499		// no-op (e.g. direct CLI use by a human operator).
500		if err := OperatorPromptMutationGuard(os.Getenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE"), b.IssueType); err != nil {
501			return err
502		}
503		// Run create hook
504		if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-create", b); err != nil {
505			return err
506		}
507	
508		return s.WithLock(func() error {
509			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
510			if err != nil {
511				return err
512			}
513			// Reject duplicate IDs
514			for _, e := range beads {
515				if e.ID == b.ID {
516					return fmt.Errorf("bead: duplicate id: %s", b.ID)
517				}
518			}
519			// Validate deps reference existing beads
520			depIDs := b.DepIDs()
521			if len(depIDs) > 0 {
522				existing := make(map[string]bool)
523				for _, e := range beads {
524					existing[e.ID] = true
525				}
526				for _, dep := range depIDs {
527					if !existing[dep] {
528						return fmt.Errorf("bead: dependency not found: %s", dep)
529					}
530				}
531			}
532			beads = append(beads, *b)
533			return s.WriteAll(beads)
534		})
535	}
536	
537	// Get retrieves a bead by ID.
538	func (s *Store) Get(id string) (*Bead, error) {
539		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
540		if err != nil {
541			return nil, err
542		}
543		for _, b := range beads {
544			if b.ID == id {
545				return &b, nil
546			}
547		}
548		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
549	}
550	
551	// Update modifies a bead by ID. The mutate function receives a pointer to modify.
552	func (s *Store) Update(id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
553		return s.WithLock(func() error {
554			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
555			if err != nil {
556				return err
557			}
558			found := false
559			for i := range beads {
560				if beads[i].ID == id {
561					mutate(&beads[i])
562					beads[i].UpdatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
563					// Core validation after mutation
564					if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
565						return err
566					}
567					// Run update hook
568					if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
569						return err
570					}
571					found = true
572					break
573				}
574			}
575			if !found {
576				return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
577			}
578			return s.WriteAll(beads)
579		})
580	}
581	
582	// HeartbeatInterval is how often a claim owner should refresh
583	// execute-loop-heartbeat-at while running. Exposed as a variable so tests
584	// can override it.
585	var HeartbeatInterval = 30 * time.Second
586	
587	// HeartbeatTTL is the maximum allowed age of execute-loop-heartbeat-at
588	// before another worker may reclaim the bead. Defaults to 3× HeartbeatInterval.
589	// Exposed as a variable so tests can override it.
590	var HeartbeatTTL = 90 * time.Second
591	
592	// Claim sets a bead to in_progress with claim metadata.
593	// Fails if the bead is already claimed (status == in_progress) with a
594	// fresh heartbeat. A bead whose execute-loop-heartbeat-at is older than
595	// HeartbeatTTL is considered stalled and will be reclaimed atomically.
596	func (s *Store) Claim(id, assignee string) error {
597		return s.ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, "", "")
598	}
599	
600	// ClaimWithOptions sets a bead to in_progress with extended claim metadata.
601	// session and worktree are optional; machine is derived from os.Hostname().
602	// A stalled in_progress bead (heartbeat older than HeartbeatTTL) is reclaimed
603	// atomically under the store's lock.
604	func (s *Store) ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error {
605		machine, _ := os.Hostname()
606		if envID := os.Getenv("DDX_MACHINE_ID"); envID != "" {
607			machine = envID
608		}
609		return s.WithLock(func() error {
610			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
611			if err != nil {
612				return err
613			}
614			for i := range beads {
615				if beads[i].ID != id {
616					continue
617				}
618				switch beads[i].Status {
619				case StatusOpen:
620					// normal claim path
621				case StatusInProgress:
622					if !heartbeatIsStale(beads[i].Extra) {
623						return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
624					}
625					// stalled claim — reclaim atomically below
626				default:
627					return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
628				}
629				// Refuse claims on beads parked with last_status=push_failed:
630				// the previous attempt landed locally but `git push` was rejected,
631				// so the local commit history exists only on this machine. Re-running
632				// primary work would duplicate or strand commits. The operator must
633				// clear execute-loop-last-status (e.g. by manually pushing or
634				// resetting) before another attempt is allowed.
635				if last, ok := beads[i].Extra["execute-loop-last-status"].(string); ok && last == "push_failed" {
636					detail, _ := beads[i].Extra["execute-loop-last-detail"].(string)
637					if detail != "" {
638						return fmt.Errorf("bead: refusing to claim %s — previous attempt landed locally but push failed (%s); clear execute-loop-last-status to retry", id, detail)
639					}
640					return fmt.Errorf("bead: refusing to claim %s — previous attempt landed locally but push failed; clear execute-loop-last-status to retry", id)
641				}
642				if beads[i].Extra == nil {
643					beads[i].Extra = make(map[string]any)
644				}
645				now := time.Now().UTC()
646				beads[i].Status = StatusInProgress
647				beads[i].Owner = assignee
648				beads[i].UpdatedAt = now
649				beads[i].Extra["claimed-at"] = now.Format(time.RFC3339)
650				beads[i].Extra["claimed-pid"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", os.Getpid())
651				beads[i].Extra["execute-loop-heartbeat-at"] = now.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
652				if machine != "" {
653					beads[i].Extra["claimed-machine"] = machine
654				}
655				if session != "" {
656					beads[i].Extra["claimed-session"] = session
657				}
658				if worktree != "" {
659					beads[i].Extra["claimed-worktree"] = worktree
660				}
661				if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
662					return err
663				}
664				if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
665					return err
666				}
667				return s.WriteAll(beads)
668			}
669			return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
670		})
671	}
672	
673	// Heartbeat refreshes execute-loop-heartbeat-at on a claimed bead. Returns
674	// an error if the bead is no longer in_progress (e.g., reclaimed by another
675	// worker), allowing the caller to stop its heartbeat loop.
676	func (s *Store) Heartbeat(id string) error {
677		return s.WithLock(func() error {
678			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
679			if err != nil {
680				return err
681			}
682			for i := range beads {
683				if beads[i].ID != id {
684					continue
685				}
686				if beads[i].Status != StatusInProgress {
687					return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot heartbeat %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
688				}
689				if beads[i].Extra == nil {
690					beads[i].Extra = make(map[string]any)
691				}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1162:{"acceptance":"ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --set closing_commit_sha=not-a-sha fails with a validation error; ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --set closing_commit_sha=\u003cshort-valid-sha\u003e stores the full commit SHA; and regression tests cover both the reject-invalid and normalize-valid repair paths","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T02:59:29Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"101584","closing_commit_sha":"3dd57abde265f3a9085ece2a0d51e3df9fa5d946","created_at":"2026-04-10T02:58:17.883733481Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:403\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit c38d7bbb2cf47baed8197c27cac19cb4adcd8644 documents ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --set closing_commit_sha=\u003csha\u003e as the supported provenance-repair path and adds a happy-path test for closed beads, but the underlying bead update implementation still routes closing_commit_sha through the generic --set handler without any normalization or verification. Unlike bead close --commit, which resolves the value through git rev-parse --verify \u003csha\u003e^{commit}, bead update accepts arbitrary strings such as not-a-sha and persists them to closed beads. That leaves FEAT-019 replay and audit provenance one bad repair away from silently storing an invalid or ambiguous commit boundary even though the docs now present this as the safe replacement for hand-editing storage.\nSuggested fix: Teach ddx bead update --set closing_commit_sha=... to use the same commit-resolution path as bead close --commit and reject invalid or non-commit values, then add a regression test proving invalid repair SHAs fail and valid abbreviated SHAs are normalized to full commits.","id":"hx-fd99632b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: validate closing_commit_sha repairs before persisting","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:02:53.028428892Z"}

File: cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:159
Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: executeOnService currently does not carry DDx-owned execution intent correctly. With the updated agent interface, DDx must pass MinPower/MaxPower and opaque passthrough constraints to Execute, then record the agent's actual model/power response. Without that, DDx cannot own bead retry escalation or audit operator constraints.
Suggested fix: Populate ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower/MaxPower and Harness/Provider/Model from the narrow passthrough envelope, capture actual model/power from Execute response/events, and persist requested_min_power/requested_max_power, actual model/power, plus requested passthrough in run and attempt evidence.
